revision.comment: Importing Wikidata [[Wikipedia:Short description|short description]]: "Shinto shrines in Kyoto, Japan" ([[Wikipedia:Shortdesc helper|Shortdesc helper]])
revision.text:
{{short description|Shinto shrines in Kyoto, Japan}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Seimei Shrine
晴明神社 | image = Seimei Shrine-3483.jpg | caption = [[Torii]] gate at entrance to Seimei Shrine | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|35|01|40|N|135|45|04|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = [[Abe no Seimei]] | founded_by = [[Emperor Ichijō]] | established = 1007 | date_destroyed = | location = 806 Ichijō-agaru, Horikawa-dōri, [[Kamigyō-ku, Kyoto|Kamigyō-ku]], [[Kyoto|Kyōto-shi]], [[Kyoto Prefecture|Kyōto-ken]]
[[〒]] 602-8222 | website = {{URL|http://www.seimeijinja.jp/}} | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = }} {{nihongo|'''Seimei Shrine'''|晴明神社|Seimei-jinja|}} is a [[Shinto shrine]] that is located in [[Kyoto]], [[Japan]]. It is dedicated to the ''[[Onmyōdō|onmyōji]]'', [[Abe no Seimei]]. ==History== The Seimei Shrine was founded on the 4th year of the [[Kankō]] [[Japanese era|era]] (1007) by [[Emperor Ichijō]], who ordered the shrine built in memory of Abe no Seimei, after his death in 1005. It was constructed on the site of Abe no Seimei's house. The main building was restored in 1925. ==Architecture== [[File:Seimei Shrine-3500.jpg|thumb|190px|left|''Seimei-i'' well with pentagram motif]] Two [[torii]] gates lead up to the entrance of the shrine. A famous well (''Seimei-i'') is located on the shrine grounds, and the water drawn from it is considered somewhat magical. Tea master [[Sen no Rikyū]] was known to have brewed tea with the water from this well. [[File:Seimei Shrine-3481.jpg|thumb|150px|right|''Ichijō Modori-bashi'']] The [[pentacle]], known locally as a ''Seimei-star'', is an important symbol found on many parts of the shrine. Abe no Seimei reputedly came up with the arcane insignia in the 10th century, symbolizing the [[Wuxing (Chinese philosophy)|Five Chinese Elements]]. [[Platycodon grandiflorus|Japanese bellflower]] (''Platycodon grandiflorus'') ornamentation are found in decorative tiles and lanterns. The five petal tips of the flower are thought to represent the same ideal as the pentagram. A nearby bridge, the ''Ichijō Modori-bashi'' is situated just south of the shrine, and is held to be a gateway between the human and spiritual realms. ==Festivals== ''[[Seimei Matsuri]]'' (晴明祭) is held every year on the [[September equinox|Autumnal equinox]]. == Images == File:Seimei Shrine-3470.jpg|''Torii'' with pentacle File:Seimei Shrine-3476.jpg|Stone lion File:Seimei Shrine-3487.jpg|Stone ''[[komainu]]'' File:Seimei Shrine-3504.jpg|[[Chōzuya]] File:Seimei Shrine-3520.jpg|Front shrine File:Seimei Shrine-3522.jpg|Peach bust File:Seimei Shrine-3535.jpg|''Kusu-no-ki'', a sacred tree File:Seimei Shrine-3555.jpg|Stone tablet for Sen no Rikyū ==See also== *[[Abe no Seimei]] *[[Onmyōdō]] ==References== {{refbegin}} {{refend}} ==External links== *[http://www.seimeijinja.jp/ Official Site] (Japanese) {{commons category|Seimei Jinja|Seimei Shrine}} {{Shinto shrine}} {{Authority control}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:Onmyōdō]] [[Category:1007 establishments]] [[Category:1000s establishments in Japan]]

晴明神社 | image = Seimei Shrine-3483.jpg | caption = [[Torii]] gate at entrance to Seimei Shrine | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|35|01|40|N|135|45|04|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = [[Abe no Seimei]] | founded_by = [[Emperor Ichijō]] | established = 1007 | date_destroyed = | location = 806 Ichijō-agaru, Horikawa-dōri, [[Kamigyō-ku, Kyoto|Kamigyō-ku]], [[Kyoto|Kyōto-shi]], [[Kyoto Prefecture|Kyōto-ken]]
[[〒]] 602-8222 | website = {{URL|http://www.seimeijinja.jp/}} | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = }} {{nihongo|'''Seimei Shrine'''|晴明神社|Seimei-jinja|}} is a [[Shinto shrine]] that is located in [[Kyoto]], [[Japan]]. It is dedicated to the ''[[Onmyōdō|onmyōji]]'', [[Abe no Seimei]]. ==History== The Seimei Shrine was founded on the 4th year of the [[Kankō]] [[Japanese era|era]] (1007) by [[Emperor Ichijō]], who ordered the shrine built in memory of Abe no Seimei, after his death in 1005. It was constructed on the site of Abe no Seimei's house. The main building was restored in 1925. ==Architecture== [[File:Seimei Shrine-3500.jpg|thumb|190px|left|''Seimei-i'' well with pentagram motif]] Two [[torii]] gates lead up to the entrance of the shrine. A famous well (''Seimei-i'') is located on the shrine grounds, and the water drawn from it is considered somewhat magical. Tea master [[Sen no Rikyū]] was known to have brewed tea with the water from this well. [[File:Seimei Shrine-3481.jpg|thumb|150px|right|''Ichijō Modori-bashi'']] The [[pentacle]], known locally as a ''Seimei-star'', is an important symbol found on many parts of the shrine. Abe no Seimei reputedly came up with the arcane insignia in the 10th century, symbolizing the [[Wuxing (Chinese philosophy)|Five Chinese Elements]]. [[Platycodon grandiflorus|Japanese bellflower]] (''Platycodon grandiflorus'') ornamentation are found in decorative tiles and lanterns. The five petal tips of the flower are thought to represent the same ideal as the pentagram. A nearby bridge, the ''Ichijō Modori-bashi'' is situated just south of the shrine, and is held to be a gateway between the human and spiritual realms. ==Festivals== ''[[Seimei Matsuri]]'' (晴明祭) is held every year on the [[September equinox|Autumnal equinox]]. == Images == File:Seimei Shrine-3470.jpg|''Torii'' with pentacle File:Seimei Shrine-3476.jpg|Stone lion File:Seimei Shrine-3487.jpg|Stone ''[[komainu]]'' File:Seimei Shrine-3504.jpg|[[Chōzuya]] File:Seimei Shrine-3520.jpg|Front shrine File:Seimei Shrine-3522.jpg|Peach bust File:Seimei Shrine-3535.jpg|''Kusu-no-ki'', a sacred tree File:Seimei Shrine-3555.jpg|Stone tablet for Sen no Rikyū ==See also== *[[Abe no Seimei]] *[[Onmyōdō]] ==References== {{refbegin}} {{refend}} ==External links== *[http://www.seimeijinja.jp/ Official Site] (Japanese) {{commons category|Seimei Jinja|Seimei Shrine}} {{Shinto shrine}} {{Authority control}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:Onmyōdō]] [[Category:1007 establishments]] [[Category:1000s establishments in Japan]]

{{short description|Shinto shrines in Kyoto, Japan}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Seimei Shrine
晴明神社 | image = Seimei Shrine-3483.jpg | caption = [[Torii]] gate at entrance to Seimei Shrine | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|35|01|40|N|135|45|04|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = [[Abe no Seimei]] | founded_by = [[Emperor Ichijō]] | established = 1007 | date_destroyed = | location = 806 Ichijō-agaru, Horikawa-dōri, [[Kamigyō-ku, Kyoto|Kamigyō-ku]], [[Kyoto|Kyōto-shi]], [[Kyoto Prefecture|Kyōto-ken]]
[[〒]] 602-8222 | website = {{URL|http://www.seimeijinja.jp/}} | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = }} {{nihongo|'''Seimei Shrine'''|晴明神社|Seimei-jinja|}} is a [[Shinto shrine]] that is located in [[Kyoto]], [[Japan]]. It is dedicated to the ''[[Onmyōdō|onmyōji]]'', [[Abe no Seimei]]. ==History== The Seimei Shrine was founded on the 4th year of the [[Kankō]] [[Japanese era|era]] (1007) by [[Emperor Ichijō]], who ordered the shrine built in memory of Abe no Seimei, after his death in 1005. It was constructed on the site of Abe no Seimei's house. The main building was restored in 1925. ==Architecture== [[File:Seimei Shrine-3500.jpg|thumb|190px|left|''Seimei-i'' well with pentagram motif]] Two [[torii]] gates lead up to the entrance of the shrine. A famous well (''Seimei-i'') is located on the shrine grounds, and the water drawn from it is considered somewhat magical. Tea master [[Sen no Rikyū]] was known to have brewed tea with the water from this well. [[File:Seimei Shrine-3481.jpg|thumb|150px|right|''Ichijō Modori-bashi'']] The [[pentacle]], known locally as a ''Seimei-star'', is an important symbol found on many parts of the shrine. Abe no Seimei reputedly came up with the arcane insignia in the 10th century, symbolizing the [[Wuxing (Chinese philosophy)|Five Chinese Elements]]. [[Platycodon grandiflorus|Japanese bellflower]] (''Platycodon grandiflorus'') ornamentation are found in decorative tiles and lanterns. The five petal tips of the flower are thought to represent the same ideal as the pentagram. A nearby bridge, the ''Ichijō Modori-bashi'' is situated just south of the shrine, and is held to be a gateway between the human and spiritual realms. ==Festivals== ''[[Seimei Matsuri]]'' (晴明祭) is held every year on the [[September equinox|Autumnal equinox]]. == Images == File:Seimei Shrine-3470.jpg|''Torii'' with pentacle File:Seimei Shrine-3476.jpg|Stone lion File:Seimei Shrine-3487.jpg|Stone ''[[komainu]]'' File:Seimei Shrine-3504.jpg|[[Chōzuya]] File:Seimei Shrine-3520.jpg|Front shrine File:Seimei Shrine-3522.jpg|Peach bust File:Seimei Shrine-3535.jpg|''Kusu-no-ki'', a sacred tree File:Seimei Shrine-3555.jpg|Stone tablet for Sen no Rikyū ==See also== *[[Abe no Seimei]] *[[Onmyōdō]] ==References== {{refbegin}} {{refend}} ==External links== *[http://www.seimeijinja.jp/ Official Site] (Japanese) {{commons category|Seimei Jinja|Seimei Shrine}} {{Shinto shrine}} {{Authority control}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:Onmyōdō]] [[Category:1007 establishments]] [[Category:1000s establishments in Japan]] [[Category:Abe clan shrines]] [[Category:Improperly tagged templates and modules2]] [[en:Seimei Shrine]][[Category:Improperly tagged templates and modules2]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia]] [[Category:Articles tagged as templates and modules]]

A nearby bridge, the ''Ichijō Modori-bashi'' is situated just south of the shrine, and is held to be a gateway between the human and spiritual realms. ==Festivals== ''[[Seimei Matsuri]]'' (晴明祭) is held every year on the [[September equinox|Autumnal equinox]]. == Images == File:Seimei Shrine-3470.jpg|''Torii'' with pentacle File:Seimei Shrine-3476.jpg|Stone lion File:Seimei Shrine-3487.jpg|Stone ''[[komainu]]'' File:Seimei Shrine-3504.jpg|[[Chōzuya]] File:Seimei Shrine-3520.jpg|Front shrine File:Seimei Shrine-3522.jpg|Peach bust File:Seimei Shrine-3535.jpg|''Kusu-no-ki'', a sacred tree File:Seimei Shrine-3555.jpg|Stone tablet for Sen no Rikyū ==See also== *[[Abe no Seimei]] *[[Onmyōdō]] ==References== {{refbegin}} {{refend}} ==External links== *[http://www.seimeijinja.jp/ Official Site] (Japanese) {{commons category|Seimei Jinja|Seimei Shrine}} {{Shinto shrine}} {{Authority control}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:Onmyōdō]] [[Category:1007 establishments]] [[Category:1000s establishments in Japan]] [[Category:Abe clan shrines]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia6]] [[en:Seimei Shrine]][[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia6]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia]] [[Category:Articles tagged as templates and modules]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia22]] [[en:Seimei Shrine]]

revision.text:
{{short description|Shinto shrines in Kyoto, Japan}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Seimei Shrine
晴明神社 | image = Seimei Shrine-3483.jpg | caption = [[Torii]] gate at entrance to Seimei Shrine | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|35|01|40|N|135|45|04|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = [[Abe no Seimei]] | founded_by = [[Emperor Ichijō]] | established = 1007 | date_destroyed = | location = 806 Ichijō-agaru, Horikawa-dōri, [[Kamigyō-ku, Kyoto|Kamigyō-ku]], [[Kyoto|Kyōto-shi]], [[Kyoto Prefecture|Kyōto-ken]]
[[〒]] 602-8222 | website = {{URL|http://www.seimeijinja.jp/}} | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = }} {{nihongo|'''Seimei Shrine'''|晴明神社|Seimei-jinja|}} is a [[Shinto shrine]] that is located in [[Kyoto]], [[Japan]]. It is dedicated to the ''[[Onmyōdō|onmyōji]]'', [[Abe no Seimei]]. ==History== The Seimei Shrine was founded on the 4th year of the [[Kankō]] [[Japanese era|era]] (1007) by [[Emperor Ichijō]], who ordered the shrine built in memory of Abe no Seimei, after his death in 1005. It was constructed on the site of Abe no Seimei's house. The main building was restored in 1925. ==Architecture== [[File:Seimei Shrine-3500.jpg|thumb|190px|left|''Seimei-i'' well with pentagram motif]] Two [[torii]] gates lead up to the entrance of the shrine. A famous well (''Seimei-i'') is located on the shrine grounds, and the water drawn from it is considered somewhat magical. Tea master [[Sen no Rikyū]] was known to have brewed tea with the water from this well. [[File:Seimei Shrine-3481.jpg|thumb|150px|right|''Ichijō Modori-bashi'']] The [[pentacle]], known locally as a ''Seimei-star'', is an important symbol found on many parts of the shrine. Abe no Seimei reputedly came up with the arcane insignia in the 10th century, symbolizing the [[Wuxing (Chinese philosophy)|Five Chinese Elements]]. [[Platycodon grandiflorus|Japanese bellflower]] (''Platycodon grandiflorus'') ornamentation are found in decorative tiles and lanterns. The five petal tips of the flower are thought to represent the same ideal as the pentagram. A nearby bridge, the ''Ichijō Modori-bashi'' is situated just south of the shrine, and is held to be a gateway between the human and spiritual realms. ==Festivals== ''[[Seimei Matsuri]]'' (晴明祭) is held every year on the [[September equinox|Autumnal equinox]]. == Images == File:Seimei Shrine-3470.jpg|''Torii'' with pentacle File:Seimei Shrine-3476.jpg|Stone lion File:Seimei Shrine-3487.jpg|Stone ''[[komainu]]'' File:Seimei Shrine-3504.jpg|[[Chōzuya]] File:Seimei Shrine-3520.jpg|Front shrine File:Seimei Shrine-3522.jpg|Peach bust File:Seimei Shrine-3535.jpg|''Kusu-no-ki'', a sacred tree File:Seimei Shrine-3555.jpg|Stone tablet for Sen no Rikyū ==See also== *[[Abe no Seimei]] *[[Onmyōdō]] ==References== {{refbegin}} {{refend}} ==External links== *[http://www.seimeijinja.jp/ Official Site] (Japanese) {{commons category|Seimei Jinja|Seimei Shrine}} {{Shinto shrine}} {{Authority control}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:Onmyōdō]] [[Category:1007 establishments]] [[Category:1000s establishments in Japan]] [[Category:Abe clan shrines]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia06]] [[en:Seimei Shrine]][[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia06]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia]] [[Category:Articles tagged as templates and modules]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia22]] [[en:Seimei Shrine]]

[[〒]] 602-8222 | website = {{URL|http://www.seimeijinja.jp/}} | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = }} {{nihongo|'''Seimei Shrine'''|晴明神社|Seimei-jinja|}} is a [[Shinto shrine]] that is located in [[Kyoto]], [[Japan]]. It is dedicated to the ''[[Onmyōdō|onmyōji]]'', [[Abe no Seimei]]. ==History== The Seimei Shrine was founded on the 4th year of the [[Kankō]] [[Japanese era|era]] (1007) by [[Emperor Ichijō]], who ordered the shrine built in memory of Abe no Seimei, after his death in 1005. It was constructed on the site of Abe no Seimei's house. The main building was restored in 1925. ==Architecture== [[File:Seimei Shrine-3500.jpg|thumb|190px|left|''Seimei-i'' well with pentagram motif]] Two [[torii]] gates lead up to the entrance of the shrine. A famous well (''Seimei-i'') is located on the shrine grounds, and the water drawn from it is considered somewhat magical. Tea master [[Sen no Rikyū]] was known to have brewed tea with the water from this well. [[File:Seimei Shrine-3481.jpg|thumb|150px|right|''Ichijō Modori-bashi'']] The [[pentacle]], known locally as a ''Seimei-star'', is an important symbol found on many parts of the shrine. Abe no Seimei reputedly came up with the arcane insignia in the 10th century, symbolizing the [[Wuxing (Chinese philosophy)|Five Chinese Elements]]. [[Platycodon grandiflorus|Japanese bellflower]] (''Platycodon grandiflorus'') ornamentation are found in decorative tiles and lanterns. The five petal tips of the flower are thought to represent the same ideal as the pentagram. A nearby bridge, the ''Ichijō Modori-bashi'' is situated just south of the shrine, and is held to be a gateway between the human and spiritual realms. ==Festivals== ''[[Seimei Matsuri]]'' (晴明祭) is held every year on the [[September equinox|Autumnal equinox]]. == Images == File:Seimei Shrine-3470.jpg|''Torii'' with pentacle File:Seimei Shrine-3476.jpg|Stone lion File:Seimei Shrine-3487.jpg|Stone ''[[komainu]]'' File:Seimei Shrine-3504.jpg|[[Chōzuya]] File:Seimei Shrine-3520.jpg|Front shrine File:Seimei Shrine-3522.jpg|Peach bust File:Seimei Shrine-3535.jpg|''Kusu-no-ki'', a sacred tree File:Seimei Shrine-3555.jpg|Stone tablet for Sen no Rikyū ==See also== *[[Abe no Seimei]] *[[Onmyōdō]] ==References== {{refbegin}} {{refend}} ==External links== *[http://www.seimeijinja.jp/ Official Site] (Japanese) {{commons category|Seimei Jinja|Seimei Shrine}} {{Shinto shrine}} {{Authority control}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:Onmyōdō]] [[Category:1007 establishments]] [[Category:1000s establishments in Japan]] [[Category:Abe clan shrines]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia06]] [[Category:dd]] [[en:Seimei Shrine]][[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia06]] [[Category:dd]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia26]] [[en:Seimei Shrine]]

A nearby bridge, the ''Ichijō Modori-bashi'' is situated just south of the shrine, and is held to be a gateway between the human and spiritual realms. ==Festivals== ''[[Seimei Matsuri]]'' (晴明祭) is held every year on the [[September equinox|Autumnal equinox]]. == images == File:Seimei Shrine-3470.jpg|''Torii'' with pentacle File:Seimei Shrine-3476.jpg|Stone lion File:Seimei Shrine-3487.jpg|Stone ''[[komainu]]'' File:Seimei Shrine-3504.jpg|[[Chōzuya]] File:Seimei Shrine-3520.jpg|Front shrine File:Seimei Shrine-3522.jpg|Peach bust File:Seimei Shrine-3535.jpg|''Kusu-no-ki'', a sacred tree File:Seimei Shrine-3555.jpg|Stone tablet for Sen no Rikyū ==See also== *[[Abe no Seimei]] *[[Onmyōdō]] ==References== {{refbegin}} {{refend}} ==External links== *[http://www.seimeijinja.jp/ Official Site] (Japanese) {{commons category|Seimei Jinja|Seimei Shrine}} {{Shinto shrine}} {{Authority control}} [[category:kk]][[category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto]] [[category:kk]][[category:Onmyōdō]] [[category:kk]][[category:1007 establishments]] [[category:kk]][[category:1000s establishments in Japan]] [[category:kk]][[category:Abe clan shrines]] [[category:kk]][[category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia]] [[category:mm]] [[en:Seimei Shrine]][[category:kk]][[category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia]] [[category:mm]] [[category:kk]][[category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia]] [[category:mm]] [[en:Seimei Shrine]]

{{short description|Shinto shrines in Kyoto, Japan}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Seimei Shrine
晴明神社 | image = Seimei Shrine-3483.jpg | caption = [[Torii]] gate at entrance to Seimei Shrine | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|35|01|40|N|135|45|04|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = [[Abe no Seimei]] | founded_by = [[Emperor Ichijō]] | established = 1007 | date_destroyed = | location = 806 Ichijō-agaru, Horikawa-dōri, [[Kamigyō-ku, Kyoto|Kamigyō-ku]], [[Kyoto|Kyōto-shi]], [[Kyoto Prefecture|Kyōto-ken]]
[[〒]] 602-8222 | website = {{URL|http://www.seimeijinja.jp/}} | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = }} {{nihongo|'''Seimei Shrine'''|晴明神社|Seimei-jinja|}} is a [[Shinto shrine]] that is located in [[Kyoto]], [[Japan]]. It is dedicated to the ''[[Onmyōdō|onmyōji]]'', [[Abe no Seimei]]. ==History== The Seimei Shrine was founded on the 4th year of the [[Kankō]] [[Japanese era|era]] (1007) by [[Emperor Ichijō]], who ordered the shrine built in memory of Abe no Seimei, after his death in 1005. It was constructed on the site of Abe no Seimei's house. The main building was restored in 1925. ==Architecture== [[File:Seimei Shrine-3500.jpg|thumb|190px|left|''Seimei-i'' well with pentagram motif]] Two [[torii]] gates lead up to the entrance of the shrine. A famous well (''Seimei-i'') is located on the shrine grounds, and the water drawn from it is considered somewhat magical. Tea master [[Sen no Rikyū]] was known to have brewed tea with the water from this well. [[File:Seimei Shrine-3481.jpg|thumb|150px|right|''Ichijō Modori-bashi'']] The [[pentacle]], known locally as a ''Seimei-star'', is an important symbol found on many parts of the shrine. Abe no Seimei reputedly came up with the arcane insignia in the 10th century, symbolizing the [[Wuxing (Chinese philosophy)|Five Chinese Elements]]. [[Platycodon grandiflorus|Japanese bellflower]] (''Platycodon grandiflorus'') ornamentation are found in decorative tiles and lanterns. The five petal tips of the flower are thought to represent the same ideal as the pentagram. A nearby bridge, the ''Ichijō Modori-bashi'' is situated just south of the shrine, and is held to be a gateway between the human and spiritual realms. ==Festivals== ''[[Seimei Matsuri]]'' (晴明祭) is held every year on the [[September equinox|Autumnal equinox]]. == images == File:Seimei Shrine-3470.jpg|''Torii'' with pentacle File:Seimei Shrine-3476.jpg|Stone lion File:Seimei Shrine-3487.jpg|Stone ''[[komainu]]'' File:Seimei Shrine-3504.jpg|[[Chōzuya]] File:Seimei Shrine-3520.jpg|Front shrine File:Seimei Shrine-3522.jpg|Peach bust File:Seimei Shrine-3535.jpg|''Kusu-no-ki'', a sacred tree File:Seimei Shrine-3555.jpg|Stone tablet for Sen no Rikyū ==See also== *[[Abe no Seimei]] *[[Onmyōdō]] ==References== {{refbegin}} {{refend}} ==External links== *[http://www.seimeijinja.jp/ Official Site] (Japanese) {{commons category|Seimei Jinja|Seimei Shrine}} {{Shinto shrine}} {{Authority control}} [[category:kk]][[category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto]] [[category:kk]][[category:Onmyōdō]] [[category:kk]][[category:1007 establishments]] [[category:kk]][[category:1000s establishments in Japan]] [[category:kk]][[category:Abe clan shrines]] [[category:kk]][[category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia]][[en:{{subst:PAGENAME]]]] [[category:mm]] [[en:Seimei Shrine]][[category:kk]][[category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia]][[en:{{subst:PAGENAME]]]] [[category:mm]] [[category:kk]][[category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia]][[en:{{subst:PAGENAME]]]] [[category:mm]] [[en:Seimei Shrine]]

A nearby bridge, the ''Ichijō Modori-bashi'' is situated just south of the shrine, and is held to be a gateway between the human and spiritual realms. ==Festivals== ''[[Seimei Matsuri]]'' (晴明祭) is held every year on the [[September equinox|Autumnal equinox]]. == images == File:Seimei Shrine-3470.jpg|''Torii'' with pentacle File:Seimei Shrine-3476.jpg|Stone lion File:Seimei Shrine-3487.jpg|Stone ''[[komainu]]'' File:Seimei Shrine-3504.jpg|[[Chōzuya]] File:Seimei Shrine-3520.jpg|Front shrine File:Seimei Shrine-3522.jpg|Peach bust File:Seimei Shrine-3535.jpg|''Kusu-no-ki'', a sacred tree File:Seimei Shrine-3555.jpg|Stone tablet for Sen no Rikyū ==See also== *[[Abe no Seimei]] *[[Onmyōdō]] ==References== {{refbegin}} {{refend}} ==External links== *[http://www.seimeijinja.jp/ Official Site] (Japanese) {{commons category|Seimei Jinja|Seimei Shrine}} {{Shinto shrine}} {{Authority control}} [[category:kk]][[category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto]] [[category:kk]][[category:Onmyōdō]] [[category:kk]][[category:1007 establishments]] [[category:kk]][[category:1000s establishments in Japan]] [[category:kk]][[category:Abe clan shrines]] [[category:kk]][[category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia]][[en:Seimei Shrine]]]] [[category:mm]] [[en:Seimei Shrine]][[category:kk]][[category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia]][[en:Seimei Shrine]]]] [[category:mm]] [[category:kk]][[category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia]][[en:Seimei Shrine]]]] [[category:mm]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Seimei Shrine]][[Category:dd]] [[Category:bump1-19]] [[Category:refreshing categories imported on 2024-01-19]] [[en:Seimei Shrine]]

[[〒]] 602-8222 | website = {{URL|http://www.seimeijinja.jp/}} | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = }} {{nihongo|'''Seimei Shrine'''|晴明神社|Seimei-jinja|}} is a [[Shinto shrine]] that is located in [[Kyoto]], [[Japan]]. It is dedicated to the ''[[Onmyōdō|onmyōji]]'', [[Abe no Seimei]]. ==History== The Seimei Shrine was founded on the 4th year of the [[Kankō]] [[Japanese era|era]] (1007) by [[Emperor Ichijō]], who ordered the shrine built in memory of Abe no Seimei, after his death in 1005. It was constructed on the site of Abe no Seimei's house. The main building was restored in 1925. ==Architecture== [[File:Seimei Shrine-3500.jpg|thumb|190px|left|''Seimei-i'' well with pentagram motif]] Two [[torii]] gates lead up to the entrance of the shrine. A famous well (''Seimei-i'') is located on the shrine grounds, and the water drawn from it is considered somewhat magical. Tea master [[Sen no Rikyū]] was known to have brewed tea with the water from this well. [[File:Seimei Shrine-3481.jpg|thumb|150px|right|''Ichijō Modori-bashi'']] The [[pentacle]], known locally as a ''Seimei-star'', is an important symbol found on many parts of the shrine. Abe no Seimei reputedly came up with the arcane insignia in the 10th century, symbolizing the [[Wuxing (Chinese philosophy)|Five Chinese Elements]]. [[Platycodon grandiflorus|Japanese bellflower]] (''Platycodon grandiflorus'') ornamentation are found in decorative tiles and lanterns. The five petal tips of the flower are thought to represent the same ideal as the pentagram. A nearby bridge, the ''Ichijō Modori-bashi'' is situated just south of the shrine, and is held to be a gateway between the human and spiritual realms. ==Festivals== ''[[Seimei Matsuri]]'' (晴明祭) is held every year on the [[September equinox|Autumnal equinox]]. == images == File:Seimei Shrine-3470.jpg|''Torii'' with pentacle File:Seimei Shrine-3476.jpg|Stone lion File:Seimei Shrine-3487.jpg|Stone ''[[komainu]]'' File:Seimei Shrine-3504.jpg|[[Chōzuya]] File:Seimei Shrine-3520.jpg|Front shrine File:Seimei Shrine-3522.jpg|Peach bust File:Seimei Shrine-3535.jpg|''Kusu-no-ki'', a sacred tree File:Seimei Shrine-3555.jpg|Stone tablet for Sen no Rikyū ==See also== *[[Abe no Seimei]] *[[Onmyōdō]] ==References== {{refbegin}} {{refend}} ==External links== *[http://www.seimeijinja.jp/ Official Site] (Japanese) {{commons category|Seimei Jinja|Seimei Shrine}} {{Shinto shrine}} [[Category:Pages that used to use Authority control]][[en:Seimei Shrine]] [[category:kk]][[category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto]] [[category:kk]][[category:Onmyōdō]] [[category:kk]][[category:1007 establishments]] [[category:kk]][[category:1000s establishments in Japan]] [[category:kk]][[category:Abe clan shrines]] [[category:kk]][[category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia]][[en:Seimei Shrine]]]] [[category:mm]] [[en:Seimei Shrine]][[category:kk]][[category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia]][[en:Seimei Shrine]]]] [[category:mm]] [[category:kk]][[category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia]][[en:Seimei Shrine]]]] [[category:mm]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Seimei Shrine]][[Category:dd]] [[Category:bump1-19]] [[Category:refreshing categories imported on 2024-01-19]] [[en:Seimei Shrine]]

A nearby bridge, the ''Ichijō Modori-bashi'' is situated just south of the shrine, and is held to be a gateway between the human and spiritual realms. ==Festivals== ''[[Seimei Matsuri]]'' (晴明祭) is held every year on the [[September equinox|Autumnal equinox]]. == images == File:Seimei Shrine-3470.jpg|''Torii'' with pentacle File:Seimei Shrine-3476.jpg|Stone lion File:Seimei Shrine-3487.jpg|Stone ''[[komainu]]'' File:Seimei Shrine-3504.jpg|[[Chōzuya]] File:Seimei Shrine-3520.jpg|Front shrine File:Seimei Shrine-3522.jpg|Peach bust File:Seimei Shrine-3535.jpg|''Kusu-no-ki'', a sacred tree File:Seimei Shrine-3555.jpg|Stone tablet for Sen no Rikyū ==See also== *[[Abe no Seimei]] *[[Onmyōdō]] ==References== {{refbegin}} {{refend}} ==External links== *[http://www.seimeijinja.jp/ Official Site] (Japanese) {{commons Category|Seimei Jinja|Seimei Shrine}} {{Shinto shrine}} [[Category:Pages that used to use Authority control]][[en:Seimei Shrine]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:Onmyōdō]] [[Category:1007 establishments]] [[Category:1000s establishments in Japan]] [[Category:Abe clan shrines]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia]][[en:Seimei Shrine]]]] [[Category:mm]] [[en:Seimei Shrine]][[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia]][[en:Seimei Shrine]]]] [[Category:mm]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia]][[en:Seimei Shrine]]]] [[Category:mm]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Seimei Shrine]][[Category:dd]] [[Category:Bump-01-21]] [[Category:Bump-01-21]] [[en:Seimei Shrine]]

{{short description|Shinto shrines in Kyoto, Japan}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Seimei Shrine
晴明神社 | image = Seimei Shrine-3483.jpg | caption = [[Torii]] gate at entrance to Seimei Shrine | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|35|01|40|N|135|45|04|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = [[Abe no Seimei]] | founded_by = [[Emperor Ichijō]] | established = 1007 | date_destroyed = | location = 806 Ichijō-agaru, Horikawa-dōri, [[Kamigyō-ku, Kyoto|Kamigyō-ku]], [[Kyoto|Kyōto-shi]], [[Kyoto Prefecture|Kyōto-ken]]
[[〒]] 602-8222 | website = {{URL|http://www.seimeijinja.jp/}} | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = }} {{nihongo|'''Seimei Shrine'''|晴明神社|Seimei-jinja|}} is a [[Shinto shrine]] that is located in [[Kyoto]], [[Japan]]. It is dedicated to the ''[[Onmyōdō|onmyōji]]'', [[Abe no Seimei]]. ==History== The Seimei Shrine was founded on the 4th year of the [[Kankō]] [[Japanese era|era]] (1007) by [[Emperor Ichijō]], who ordered the shrine built in memory of Abe no Seimei, after his death in 1005. It was constructed on the site of Abe no Seimei's house. The main building was restored in 1925. ==Architecture== [[File:Seimei Shrine-3500.jpg|thumb|190px|left|''Seimei-i'' well with pentagram motif]] Two [[torii]] gates lead up to the entrance of the shrine. A famous well (''Seimei-i'') is located on the shrine grounds, and the water drawn from it is considered somewhat magical. Tea master [[Sen no Rikyū]] was known to have brewed tea with the water from this well. [[File:Seimei Shrine-3481.jpg|thumb|150px|right|''Ichijō Modori-bashi'']] The [[pentacle]], known locally as a ''Seimei-star'', is an important symbol found on many parts of the shrine. Abe no Seimei reputedly came up with the arcane insignia in the 10th century, symbolizing the [[Wuxing (Chinese philosophy)|Five Chinese Elements]]. [[Platycodon grandiflorus|Japanese bellflower]] (''Platycodon grandiflorus'') ornamentation are found in decorative tiles and lanterns. The five petal tips of the flower are thought to represent the same ideal as the pentagram. A nearby bridge, the ''Ichijō Modori-bashi'' is situated just south of the shrine, and is held to be a gateway between the human and spiritual realms. ==Festivals== ''[[Seimei Matsuri]]'' (晴明祭) is held every year on the [[September equinox|Autumnal equinox]]. == images == File:Seimei Shrine-3470.jpg|''Torii'' with pentacle File:Seimei Shrine-3476.jpg|Stone lion File:Seimei Shrine-3487.jpg|Stone ''[[komainu]]'' File:Seimei Shrine-3504.jpg|[[Chōzuya]] File:Seimei Shrine-3520.jpg|Front shrine File:Seimei Shrine-3522.jpg|Peach bust File:Seimei Shrine-3535.jpg|''Kusu-no-ki'', a sacred tree File:Seimei Shrine-3555.jpg|Stone tablet for Sen no Rikyū ==See also== *[[Abe no Seimei]] *[[Onmyōdō]] ==References== {{refbegin}} {{refend}} ==External links== *[http://www.seimeijinja.jp/ Official Site] (Japanese) {{commons Category|Seimei Jinja|Seimei Shrine}} {{Shinto shrine}} [[Category:Pages that used to use Authority control]][[en:Seimei Shrine]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:Onmyōdō]] [[Category:1007 establishments]] [[Category:1000s establishments in Japan]] [[Category:Abe clan shrines]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia]][[en:Seimei Shrine]]]] [[Category:mm]] [[en:Seimei Shrine]][[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia]][[en:Seimei Shrine]]]] [[Category:mm]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia]][[en:Seimei Shrine]]]] [[Category:mm]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Seimei Shrine]][[Category:dd]] [[Category:Bump-01-21]] [[Category:Bump-01-21]] [[Category:bump 1-26]] [[en:Seimei Shrine]]

[[〒]] 602-8222 | website = {{URL|http://www.seimeijinja.jp/}} | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = }} {{nihongo|'''Seimei Shrine'''|晴明神社|Seimei-jinja|}} is a [[Shinto shrine]] that is located in [[Kyoto]], [[Japan]]. It is dedicated to the ''[[Onmyōdō|onmyōji]]'', [[Abe no Seimei]]. ==History== The Seimei Shrine was founded on the 4th year of the [[Kankō]] [[Japanese era|era]] (1007) by [[Emperor Ichijō]], who ordered the shrine built in memory of Abe no Seimei, after his death in 1005. It was constructed on the site of Abe no Seimei's house. The main building was restored in 1925. ==Architecture== [[File:Seimei Shrine-3500.jpg|thumb|190px|left|''Seimei-i'' well with pentagram motif]] Two [[torii]] gates lead up to the entrance of the shrine. A famous well (''Seimei-i'') is located on the shrine grounds, and the water drawn from it is considered somewhat magical. Tea master [[Sen no Rikyū]] was known to have brewed tea with the water from this well. [[File:Seimei Shrine-3481.jpg|thumb|150px|right|''Ichijō Modori-bashi'']] The [[pentacle]], known locally as a ''Seimei-star'', is an important symbol found on many parts of the shrine. Abe no Seimei reputedly came up with the arcane insignia in the 10th century, symbolizing the [[Wuxing (Chinese philosophy)|Five Chinese Elements]]. [[Platycodon grandiflorus|Japanese bellflower]] (''Platycodon grandiflorus'') ornamentation are found in decorative tiles and lanterns. The five petal tips of the flower are thought to represent the same ideal as the pentagram. A nearby bridge, the ''Ichijō Modori-bashi'' is situated just south of the shrine, and is held to be a gateway between the human and spiritual realms. ==Festivals== ''[[Seimei Matsuri]]'' (晴明祭) is held every year on the [[September equinox|Autumnal equinox]]. == images == File:Seimei Shrine-3470.jpg|''Torii'' with pentacle File:Seimei Shrine-3476.jpg|Stone lion File:Seimei Shrine-3487.jpg|Stone ''[[komainu]]'' File:Seimei Shrine-3504.jpg|[[Chōzuya]] File:Seimei Shrine-3520.jpg|Front shrine File:Seimei Shrine-3522.jpg|Peach bust File:Seimei Shrine-3535.jpg|''Kusu-no-ki'', a sacred tree File:Seimei Shrine-3555.jpg|Stone tablet for Sen no Rikyū ==See also== *[[Abe no Seimei]] *[[Onmyōdō]] ==References== {{refbegin}} {{refend}} ==External links== *[http://www.seimeijinja.jp/ Official Site] (Japanese) {{commons Category|Seimei Jinja|Seimei Shrine}} {{Shinto shrine}} [[Category:Pages that used to use Authority control]][[en:Seimei Shrine]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:Onmyōdō]] [[Category:1007 establishments]] [[Category:1000s establishments in Japan]] [[Category:Abe clan shrines]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 1 6 4]][[en:Seimei Shrine]]]] [[Category:mm]] [[en:Seimei Shrine]][[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 1 6 4]][[en:Seimei Shrine]]]] [[Category:mm]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 1 6 4]][[en:Seimei Shrine]]]] [[Category:mm]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Seimei Shrine]][[Category:dd]] [[Category:Bump-01-21]] [[Category:Bump-01-21]] [[Category:bump 1-26]] [[Category:qq]][[en:Seimei Shrine}][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[en:Seimei Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:kk]][[en:Seimei Shrine]][[Category:kk]] [[en:Seimei Shrine]]

A nearby bridge, the ''Ichijō Modori-bashi'' is situated just south of the shrine, and is held to be a gateway between the human and spiritual realms. ==Festivals== ''[[Seimei Matsuri]]'' (晴明祭) is held every year on the [[September equinox|Autumnal equinox]]. == images == File:Seimei Shrine-3470.jpg|''Torii'' with pentacle File:Seimei Shrine-3476.jpg|Stone lion File:Seimei Shrine-3487.jpg|Stone ''[[komainu]]'' File:Seimei Shrine-3504.jpg|[[Chōzuya]] File:Seimei Shrine-3520.jpg|Front shrine File:Seimei Shrine-3522.jpg|Peach bust File:Seimei Shrine-3535.jpg|''Kusu-no-ki'', a sacred tree File:Seimei Shrine-3555.jpg|Stone tablet for Sen no Rikyū ==See also== *[[Abe no Seimei]] *[[Onmyōdō]] ==References== {{refbegin}} {{refend}} ==External links== *[http://www.seimeijinja.jp/ Official Site] (Japanese) {{commons Category|Seimei Jinja|Seimei Shrine}} {{Shinto shrine}} [[Category:Pages that used to use Authority control]][[en:Seimei Shrine]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:Onmyōdō]] [[Category:1007 establishments]] [[Category:1000s establishments in Japan]] [[Category:Abe clan shrines]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 1]][[en:Seimei Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[en:Seimei Shrine]]]] [[Category:mm]] [[en:Seimei Shrine]][[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 1]][[en:Seimei Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[en:Seimei Shrine]]]] [[Category:mm]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 1]][[en:Seimei Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[en:Seimei Shrine]]]] [[Category:mm]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Seimei Shrine]][[Category:dd]] [[Category:Bump-01-21]] [[Category:Bump-01-21]] [[Category:bump 1-26]] [[Category:Seimei Shrines]] [[en:Seimei Shrine}][[en:Seimei Shrine]][[en:Seimei Shrine]][[en:Seimei Shrine]]

{{short description|Shinto shrines in Kyoto, Japan}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Seimei Shrine
晴明神社 | image = Seimei Shrine-3483.jpg | caption = [[Torii]] gate at entrance to Seimei Shrine | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|35|01|40|N|135|45|04|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = [[Abe no Seimei]] | founded_by = [[Emperor Ichijō]] | established = 1007 | date_destroyed = | location = 806 Ichijō-agaru, Horikawa-dōri, [[Kamigyō-ku, Kyoto|Kamigyō-ku]], [[Kyoto|Kyōto-shi]], [[Kyoto Prefecture|Kyōto-ken]]
[[〒]] 602-8222 | website = {{URL|http://www.seimeijinja.jp/}} | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = }} {{nihongo|'''Seimei Shrine'''|晴明神社|Seimei-jinja|}} is a [[Shinto shrine]] that is located in [[Kyoto]], [[Japan]]. It is dedicated to the ''[[Onmyōdō|onmyōji]]'', [[Abe no Seimei]]. ==History== The Seimei Shrine was founded on the 4th year of the [[Kankō]] [[Japanese era|era]] (1007) by [[Emperor Ichijō]], who ordered the shrine built in memory of Abe no Seimei, after his death in 1005. It was constructed on the site of Abe no Seimei's house. The main building was restored in 1925. ==Architecture== [[File:Seimei Shrine-3500.jpg|thumb|190px|left|''Seimei-i'' well with pentagram motif]] Two [[torii]] gates lead up to the entrance of the shrine. A famous well (''Seimei-i'') is located on the shrine grounds, and the water drawn from it is considered somewhat magical. Tea master [[Sen no Rikyū]] was known to have brewed tea with the water from this well. [[File:Seimei Shrine-3481.jpg|thumb|150px|right|''Ichijō Modori-bashi'']] The [[pentacle]], known locally as a ''Seimei-star'', is an important symbol found on many parts of the shrine. Abe no Seimei reputedly came up with the arcane insignia in the 10th century, symbolizing the [[Wuxing (Chinese philosophy)|Five Chinese Elements]]. [[Platycodon grandiflorus|Japanese bellflower]] (''Platycodon grandiflorus'') ornamentation are found in decorative tiles and lanterns. The five petal tips of the flower are thought to represent the same ideal as the pentagram. A nearby bridge, the ''Ichijō Modori-bashi'' is situated just south of the shrine, and is held to be a gateway between the human and spiritual realms. ==Festivals== ''[[Seimei Matsuri]]'' (晴明祭) is held every year on the [[September equinox|Autumnal equinox]]. == images == File:Seimei Shrine-3470.jpg|''Torii'' with pentacle File:Seimei Shrine-3476.jpg|Stone lion File:Seimei Shrine-3487.jpg|Stone ''[[komainu]]'' File:Seimei Shrine-3504.jpg|[[Chōzuya]] File:Seimei Shrine-3520.jpg|Front shrine File:Seimei Shrine-3522.jpg|Peach bust File:Seimei Shrine-3535.jpg|''Kusu-no-ki'', a sacred tree File:Seimei Shrine-3555.jpg|Stone tablet for Sen no Rikyū ==See also== *[[Abe no Seimei]] *[[Onmyōdō]] ==References== {{refbegin}} {{refend}} ==External links== *[http://www.seimeijinja.jp/ Official Site] (Japanese) {{commons Category|Seimei Jinja|Seimei Shrine}} {{Shinto shrine}} [[Category:Pages that used to use Authority control]][[en:Seimei Shrine]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:Onmyōdō]] [[Category:1007 establishments]] [[Category:1000s establishments in Japan]] [[Category:Abe clan shrines]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 1]] [[Category:qq--qq]] [[en:Seimei Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[en:Seimei Shrine]]]] [[Category:mm]] [[en:Seimei Shrine]][[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 1]] [[Category:qq--qq]] [[en:Seimei Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[en:Seimei Shrine]]]] [[Category:mm]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 1]] [[Category:qq--qq]] [[en:Seimei Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[en:Seimei Shrine]]]] [[Category:mm]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Seimei Shrine]][[Category:dd]] [[Category:Bump-01-21]] [[Category:Bump-01-21]] [[Category:bump 1-26]] [[Category:Seimei Shrines]] [[en:Seimei Shrine}][[en:Seimei Shrine]][[en:Seimei Shrine]][[en:Seimei Shrine]]

[[Platycodon grandiflorus|Japanese bellflower]] (''Platycodon grandiflorus'') ornamentation are found in decorative tiles and lanterns. The five petal tips of the flower are thought to represent the same ideal as the pentagram. A nearby bridge, the ''Ichijō Modori-bashi'' is situated just south of the shrine, and is held to be a gateway between the human and spiritual realms. ==Festivals== ''[[Seimei Matsuri]]'' (晴明祭) is held every year on the [[September equinox|Autumnal equinox]]. == images == File:Seimei Shrine-3470.jpg|''Torii'' with pentacle File:Seimei Shrine-3476.jpg|Stone lion File:Seimei Shrine-3487.jpg|Stone ''[[komainu]]'' File:Seimei Shrine-3504.jpg|[[Chōzuya]] File:Seimei Shrine-3520.jpg|Front shrine File:Seimei Shrine-3522.jpg|Peach bust File:Seimei Shrine-3535.jpg|''Kusu-no-ki'', a sacred tree File:Seimei Shrine-3555.jpg|Stone tablet for Sen no Rikyū ==See also== *[[Abe no Seimei]] *[[Onmyōdō]] ==References== {{refbegin}} {{refend}} ==External links== *[http://www.seimeijinja.jp/ Official Site] (Japanese) {{commons Category|Seimei Jinja|Seimei Shrine}} {{Shinto shrine}} [[Category:Pages that used to use Authority control]][[en:Seimei Shrine]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:Onmyōdō]] [[Category:1007 establishments]] [[Category:1000s establishments in Japan]] [[Category:Abe clan shrines]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 1]][[Category:qq 1]] [[Category:qq--qq]] [[en:Seimei Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[en:Seimei Shrine]]]] [[Category:mm]] [[en:Seimei Shrine]][[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 1]][[Category:qq 1]] [[Category:qq--qq]] [[en:Seimei Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[en:Seimei Shrine]]]] [[Category:mm]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 1]][[Category:qq 1]] [[Category:qq--qq]] [[en:Seimei Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[en:Seimei Shrine]]]] [[Category:mm]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Seimei Shrine]][[Category:dd]] [[Category:Bump-01-21]] [[Category:Bump-01-21]] [[Category:bump 1-26]] [[Category:Seimei Shrines]] [[en:Seimei Shrine}][[en:Seimei Shrine]][[en:Seimei Shrine]][[en:Seimei Shrine]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]]

{{short description|Shinto shrines in Kyoto, Japan}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Seimei Shrine
晴明神社 | image = Seimei Shrine-3483.jpg | caption = [[Torii]] gate at entrance to Seimei Shrine | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|35|01|40|N|135|45|04|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = [[Abe no Seimei]] | founded_by = [[Emperor Ichijō]] | established = 1007 | date_destroyed = | location = 806 Ichijō-agaru, Horikawa-dōri, [[Kamigyō-ku, Kyoto|Kamigyō-ku]], [[Kyoto|Kyōto-shi]], [[Kyoto Prefecture|Kyōto-ken]]
[[〒]] 602-8222 | website = {{URL|http://www.seimeijinja.jp/}} | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = }} {{nihongo|'''Seimei Shrine'''|晴明神社|Seimei-jinja|}} is a [[Shinto shrine]] that is located in [[Kyoto]], [[Japan]]. It is dedicated to the ''[[Onmyōdō|onmyōji]]'', [[Abe no Seimei]]. ==History== The Seimei Shrine was founded on the 4th year of the [[Kankō]] [[Japanese era|era]] (1007) by [[Emperor Ichijō]], who ordered the shrine built in memory of Abe no Seimei, after his death in 1005. It was constructed on the site of Abe no Seimei's house. The main building was restored in 1925. ==Architecture== [[File:Seimei Shrine-3500.jpg|thumb|190px|left|''Seimei-i'' well with pentagram motif]] Two [[torii]] gates lead up to the entrance of the shrine. A famous well (''Seimei-i'') is located on the shrine grounds, and the water drawn from it is considered somewhat magical. Tea master [[Sen no Rikyū]] was known to have brewed tea with the water from this well. [[File:Seimei Shrine-3481.jpg|thumb|150px|right|''Ichijō Modori-bashi'']] The [[pentacle]], known locally as a ''Seimei-star'', is an important symbol found on many parts of the shrine. Abe no Seimei reputedly came up with the arcane insignia in the 10th century, symbolizing the [[Wuxing (Chinese philosophy)|Five Chinese Elements]]. [[Platycodon grandiflorus|Japanese bellflower]] (''Platycodon grandiflorus'') ornamentation are found in decorative tiles and lanterns. The five petal tips of the flower are thought to represent the same ideal as the pentagram. A nearby bridge, the ''Ichijō Modori-bashi'' is situated just south of the shrine, and is held to be a gateway between the human and spiritual realms. ==Festivals== ''[[Seimei Matsuri]]'' (晴明祭) is held every year on the [[September equinox|Autumnal equinox]]. == images == File:Seimei Shrine-3470.jpg|''Torii'' with pentacle File:Seimei Shrine-3476.jpg|Stone lion File:Seimei Shrine-3487.jpg|Stone ''[[komainu]]'' File:Seimei Shrine-3504.jpg|[[Chōzuya]] File:Seimei Shrine-3520.jpg|Front shrine File:Seimei Shrine-3522.jpg|Peach bust File:Seimei Shrine-3535.jpg|''Kusu-no-ki'', a sacred tree File:Seimei Shrine-3555.jpg|Stone tablet for Sen no Rikyū ==See also== *[[Abe no Seimei]] *[[Onmyōdō]] ==References== {{refbegin}} {{refend}} ==External links== *[http://www.seimeijinja.jp/ Official Site] (Japanese) {{commons Category|Seimei Jinja|Seimei Shrine}} {{Shinto shrine}} [[Category:Pages that used to use Authority control]][[en:Seimei Shrine]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:Onmyōdō]] [[Category:1007 establishments]] [[Category:1000s establishments in Japan]] [[Category:Abe clan shrines]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 1]][[Category:qq 1]] [[Category:qq--qq]] [[en:Seimei Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[en:Seimei Shrine]]]] [[Category:mm]] [[en:Seimei Shrine]][[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 1]][[Category:qq 1]] [[Category:qq--qq]] [[en:Seimei Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[en:Seimei Shrine]]]] [[Category:mm]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 1]][[Category:qq 1]] [[Category:qq--qq]] [[en:Seimei Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[en:Seimei Shrine]]]] [[Category:mm]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Seimei Shrine]][[Category:dd]] [[Category:Bump-01-21]] [[Category:Bump-01-21]] [[Category:bump 1-26]] [[Category:Seimei Shrines]] [[en:Seimei Shrine]][[en:Seimei Shrine]][[en:Seimei Shrine]][[en:Seimei Shrine]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]]

{{short description|Shinto shrines in Kyoto, Japan}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Seimei Shrine
晴明神社 | image = Seimei Shrine-3483.jpg | caption = [[Torii]] gate at entrance to Seimei Shrine | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|35|01|40|N|135|45|04|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = [[Abe no Seimei]] | founded_by = [[Emperor Ichijō]] | established = 1007 | date_destroyed = | location = 806 Ichijō-agaru, Horikawa-dōri, [[Kamigyō-ku, Kyoto|Kamigyō-ku]], [[Kyoto|Kyōto-shi]], [[Kyoto Prefecture|Kyōto-ken]]
[[〒]] 602-8222 | website = {{URL|http://www.seimeijinja.jp/}} | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = }} {{nihongo|'''Seimei Shrine'''|晴明神社|Seimei-jinja|}} is a [[Shinto shrine]] that is located in [[Kyoto]], [[Japan]]. It is dedicated to the ''[[Onmyōdō|onmyōji]]'', [[Abe no Seimei]]. ==History== The Seimei Shrine was founded on the 4th year of the [[Kankō]] [[Japanese era|era]] (1007) by [[Emperor Ichijō]], who ordered the shrine built in memory of Abe no Seimei, after his death in 1005. It was constructed on the site of Abe no Seimei's house. The main building was restored in 1925. ==Architecture== [[File:Seimei Shrine-3500.jpg|thumb|190px|left|''Seimei-i'' well with pentagram motif]] Two [[torii]] gates lead up to the entrance of the shrine. A famous well (''Seimei-i'') is located on the shrine grounds, and the water drawn from it is considered somewhat magical. Tea master [[Sen no Rikyū]] was known to have brewed tea with the water from this well. [[File:Seimei Shrine-3481.jpg|thumb|150px|right|''Ichijō Modori-bashi'']] The [[pentacle]], known locally as a ''Seimei-star'', is an important symbol found on many parts of the shrine. Abe no Seimei reputedly came up with the arcane insignia in the 10th century, symbolizing the [[Wuxing (Chinese philosophy)|Five Chinese Elements]]. [[Platycodon grandiflorus|Japanese bellflower]] (''Platycodon grandiflorus'') ornamentation are found in decorative tiles and lanterns. The five petal tips of the flower are thought to represent the same ideal as the pentagram. A nearby bridge, the ''Ichijō Modori-bashi'' is situated just south of the shrine, and is held to be a gateway between the human and spiritual realms. ==Festivals== ''[[Seimei Matsuri]]'' (晴明祭) is held every year on the [[September equinox|Autumnal equinox]]. == images == File:Seimei Shrine-3470.jpg|''Torii'' with pentacle File:Seimei Shrine-3476.jpg|Stone lion File:Seimei Shrine-3487.jpg|Stone ''[[komainu]]'' File:Seimei Shrine-3504.jpg|[[Chōzuya]] File:Seimei Shrine-3520.jpg|Front shrine File:Seimei Shrine-3522.jpg|Peach bust File:Seimei Shrine-3535.jpg|''Kusu-no-ki'', a sacred tree File:Seimei Shrine-3555.jpg|Stone tablet for Sen no Rikyū ==See also== *[[Abe no Seimei]] *[[Onmyōdō]] ==References== {{refbegin}} {{refend}} ==External links== *[http://www.seimeijinja.jp/ Official Site] (Japanese) {{commons Category|Seimei Jinja|Seimei Shrine}} {{Shinto shrine}} [[Category:Pages that used to use Authority control]][[en:Seimei Shrine]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:Onmyōdō]] [[Category:1007 establishments]] [[Category:1000s establishments in Japan]] [[Category:Abe clan shrines]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 1]][[Category:qq 1]] [[en:Seimei Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[en:Seimei Shrine]]]] [[Category:mm]] [[en:Seimei Shrine]][[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 1]][[Category:qq 1]] [[en:Seimei Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[en:Seimei Shrine]]]] [[Category:mm]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 1]][[Category:qq 1]] [[en:Seimei Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[en:Seimei Shrine]]]] [[Category:mm]] [[Category:Seimei Shrine]][[Category:Bump-01-21]] [[Category:Bump-01-21]] [[Category:bump 1-26]] [[Category:Seimei Shrines]] [[en:Seimei Shrine]][[en:Seimei Shrine]][[en:Seimei Shrine]][[en:Seimei Shrine]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Seimei Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]]

A nearby bridge, the ''Ichijō Modori-bashi'' is situated just south of the shrine, and is held to be a gateway between the human and spiritual realms. ==Festivals== ''[[Seimei Matsuri]]'' (晴明祭) is held every year on the [[September equinox|Autumnal equinox]]. == images == File:Seimei Shrine-3470.jpg|''Torii'' with pentacle File:Seimei Shrine-3476.jpg|Stone lion File:Seimei Shrine-3487.jpg|Stone ''[[komainu]]'' File:Seimei Shrine-3504.jpg|[[Chōzuya]] File:Seimei Shrine-3520.jpg|Front shrine File:Seimei Shrine-3522.jpg|Peach bust File:Seimei Shrine-3535.jpg|''Kusu-no-ki'', a sacred tree File:Seimei Shrine-3555.jpg|Stone tablet for Sen no Rikyū ==See also== *[[Abe no Seimei]] *[[Onmyōdō]] ==References== {{refbegin}} {{refend}} ==External links== *[http://www.seimeijinja.jp/ Official Site] (Japanese) {{commons Category|Seimei Jinja|Seimei Shrine}} {{Shinto shrine}} [[Category:Pages that used to use Authority control]][[en:Seimei Shrine]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:Onmyōdō]] [[Category:1007 establishments]] [[Category:1000s establishments in Japan]] [[Category:Abe clan shrines]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 1]][[en:Seimei Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[en:Seimei Shrine]]]] [[Category:mm]] [[en:Seimei Shrine]][[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 1]][[en:Seimei Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[en:Seimei Shrine]]]] [[Category:mm]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 1]][[en:Seimei Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[en:Seimei Shrine]]]] [[Category:mm]] [[Category:Seimei Shrine]][[Category:bump 1-26]] [[Category:Seimei Shrines]] [[en:Seimei Shrine]][[en:Seimei Shrine]][[en:Seimei Shrine]][[en:Seimei Shrine]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Seimei Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Seimei Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:New]]

{{short description|Shinto shrines in Kyoto, Japan}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Seimei Shrine
晴明神社 | image = Seimei Shrine-3483.jpg | caption = [[Torii]] gate at entrance to Seimei Shrine | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|35|01|40|N|135|45|04|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = [[Abe no Seimei]] | founded_by = [[Emperor Ichijō]] | established = 1007 | date_destroyed = | location = 806 Ichijō-agaru, Horikawa-dōri, [[Kamigyō-ku, Kyoto|Kamigyō-ku]], [[Kyoto|Kyōto-shi]], [[Kyoto Prefecture|Kyōto-ken]]
[[〒]] 602-8222 | website = {{URL|http://www.seimeijinja.jp/}} | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = }} {{nihongo|'''Seimei Shrine'''|晴明神社|Seimei-jinja|}} is a [[Shinto shrine]] that is located in [[Kyoto]], [[Japan]]. It is dedicated to the ''[[Onmyōdō|onmyōji]]'', [[Abe no Seimei]]. ==History== The Seimei Shrine was founded on the 4th year of the [[Kankō]] [[Japanese era|era]] (1007) by [[Emperor Ichijō]], who ordered the shrine built in memory of Abe no Seimei, after his death in 1005. It was constructed on the site of Abe no Seimei's house. The main building was restored in 1925. ==Architecture== [[File:Seimei Shrine-3500.jpg|thumb|190px|left|''Seimei-i'' well with pentagram motif]] Two [[torii]] gates lead up to the entrance of the shrine. A famous well (''Seimei-i'') is located on the shrine grounds, and the water drawn from it is considered somewhat magical. Tea master [[Sen no Rikyū]] was known to have brewed tea with the water from this well. [[File:Seimei Shrine-3481.jpg|thumb|150px|right|''Ichijō Modori-bashi'']] The [[pentacle]], known locally as a ''Seimei-star'', is an important symbol found on many parts of the shrine. Abe no Seimei reputedly came up with the arcane insignia in the 10th century, symbolizing the [[Wuxing (Chinese philosophy)|Five Chinese Elements]]. [[Platycodon grandiflorus|Japanese bellflower]] (''Platycodon grandiflorus'') ornamentation are found in decorative tiles and lanterns. The five petal tips of the flower are thought to represent the same ideal as the pentagram. A nearby bridge, the ''Ichijō Modori-bashi'' is situated just south of the shrine, and is held to be a gateway between the human and spiritual realms. ==Festivals== ''[[Seimei Matsuri]]'' (晴明祭) is held every year on the [[September equinox|Autumnal equinox]]. == images == File:Seimei Shrine-3470.jpg|''Torii'' with pentacle File:Seimei Shrine-3476.jpg|Stone lion File:Seimei Shrine-3487.jpg|Stone ''[[komainu]]'' File:Seimei Shrine-3504.jpg|[[Chōzuya]] File:Seimei Shrine-3520.jpg|Front shrine File:Seimei Shrine-3522.jpg|Peach bust File:Seimei Shrine-3535.jpg|''Kusu-no-ki'', a sacred tree File:Seimei Shrine-3555.jpg|Stone tablet for Sen no Rikyū ==See also== *[[Abe no Seimei]] *[[Onmyōdō]] ==References== {{refbegin}} {{refend}} ==External links== *[http://www.seimeijinja.jp/ Official Site] (Japanese) {{commons Category|Seimei Jinja|Seimei Shrine}} {{Shinto shrine}} [[Category:Pages that used to use Authority control]][[en:Seimei Shrine]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:Onmyōdō]] [[Category:1007 establishments]] [[Category:1000s establishments in Japan]] [[Category:Abe clan shrines]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 1]][[en:Seimei Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[en:Seimei Shrine]]]] [[Category:mm]] [[en:Seimei Shrine]][[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 1]][[en:Seimei Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[en:Seimei Shrine]]]] [[Category:mm]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 1]][[en:Seimei Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[en:Seimei Shrine]]]] [[Category:mm]] [[Category:Seimei Shrine]][[Category:bump 1-26]] [[Category:Seimei Shrines]] [[en:Seimei Shrine]][[en:Seimei Shrine]][[en:Seimei Shrine]][[en:Seimei Shrine]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Seimei Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Seimei Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]]

It is dedicated to the ''[[Onmyōdō|onmyōji]]'', [[Abe no Seimei]]. ==History== The Seimei Shrine was founded on the 4th year of the [[Kankō]] [[Japanese era|era]] (1007) by [[Emperor Ichijō]], who ordered the shrine built in memory of Abe no Seimei, after his death in 1005. It was constructed on the site of Abe no Seimei's house. The main building was restored in 1925. ==Architecture== [[File:Seimei Shrine-3500.jpg|thumb|190px|left|''Seimei-i'' well with pentagram motif]] Two [[torii]] gates lead up to the entrance of the shrine. A famous well (''Seimei-i'') is located on the shrine grounds, and the water drawn from it is considered somewhat magical. Tea master [[Sen no Rikyū]] was known to have brewed tea with the water from this well. [[File:Seimei Shrine-3481.jpg|thumb|150px|right|''Ichijō Modori-bashi'']] The [[pentacle]], known locally as a ''Seimei-star'', is an important symbol found on many parts of the shrine. Abe no Seimei reputedly came up with the arcane insignia in the 10th century, symbolizing the [[Wuxing (Chinese philosophy)|Five Chinese Elements]]. [[Platycodon grandiflorus|Japanese bellflower]] (''Platycodon grandiflorus'') ornamentation are found in decorative tiles and lanterns. The five petal tips of the flower are thought to represent the same ideal as the pentagram. A nearby bridge, the ''Ichijō Modori-bashi'' is situated just south of the shrine, and is held to be a gateway between the human and spiritual realms. ==Festivals== ''[[Seimei Matsuri]]'' (晴明祭) is held every year on the [[September equinox|Autumnal equinox]]. == images == File:Seimei Shrine-3470.jpg|''Torii'' with pentacle File:Seimei Shrine-3476.jpg|Stone lion File:Seimei Shrine-3487.jpg|Stone ''[[komainu]]'' File:Seimei Shrine-3504.jpg|[[Chōzuya]] File:Seimei Shrine-3520.jpg|Front shrine File:Seimei Shrine-3522.jpg|Peach bust File:Seimei Shrine-3535.jpg|''Kusu-no-ki'', a sacred tree File:Seimei Shrine-3555.jpg|Stone tablet for Sen no Rikyū ==See also== *[[Abe no Seimei]] *[[Onmyōdō]] ==References== {{refbegin}} {{refend}} ==External links== *[http://www.seimeijinja.jp/ Official Site] (Japanese) {{commons Category|Seimei Jinja|Seimei Shrine}} {{Shinto shrine}} ]] ]] ]] {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages that used to use Authority control]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:Onmyōdō]] [[Category:1007 establishments]] [[Category:1000s establishments in Japan]] [[Category:Abe clan shrines]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 1]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:mm]] [[Category:Seimei Shrine]] [[Category:bump 1-26]] [[Category:Seimei Shrines]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia]] }} [[en:Seimei Shrine]] [[sn:Seimei Shrine]]

{{short description|Shinto shrines in Kyoto, Japan}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Seimei Shrine
晴明神社 | image = Seimei Shrine-3483.jpg | caption = [[Torii]] gate at entrance to Seimei Shrine | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|35|01|40|N|135|45|04|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = [[Abe no Seimei]] | founded_by = [[Emperor Ichijō]] | established = 1007 | date_destroyed = | location = 806 Ichijō-agaru, Horikawa-dōri, [[Kamigyō-ku, Kyoto|Kamigyō-ku]], [[Kyoto|Kyōto-shi]], [[Kyoto Prefecture|Kyōto-ken]]
[[〒]] 602-8222 | website = {{URL|http://www.seimeijinja.jp/}} | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = }} {{nihongo|'''Seimei Shrine'''|晴明神社|Seimei-jinja|}} is a [[Shinto shrine]] that is located in [[Kyoto]], [[Japan]]. It is dedicated to the ''[[Onmyōdō|onmyōji]]'', [[Abe no Seimei]]. ==History== The Seimei Shrine was founded on the 4th year of the [[Kankō]] [[Japanese era|era]] (1007) by [[Emperor Ichijō]], who ordered the shrine built in memory of Abe no Seimei, after his death in 1005. It was constructed on the site of Abe no Seimei's house. The main building was restored in 1925. ==Architecture== [[File:Seimei Shrine-3500.jpg|thumb|190px|left|''Seimei-i'' well with pentagram motif]] Two [[torii]] gates lead up to the entrance of the shrine. A famous well (''Seimei-i'') is located on the shrine grounds, and the water drawn from it is considered somewhat magical. Tea master [[Sen no Rikyū]] was known to have brewed tea with the water from this well. [[File:Seimei Shrine-3481.jpg|thumb|150px|right|''Ichijō Modori-bashi'']] The [[pentacle]], known locally as a ''Seimei-star'', is an important symbol found on many parts of the shrine. Abe no Seimei reputedly came up with the arcane insignia in the 10th century, symbolizing the [[Wuxing (Chinese philosophy)|Five Chinese Elements]]. [[Platycodon grandiflorus|Japanese bellflower]] (''Platycodon grandiflorus'') ornamentation are found in decorative tiles and lanterns. The five petal tips of the flower are thought to represent the same ideal as the pentagram. A nearby bridge, the ''Ichijō Modori-bashi'' is situated just south of the shrine, and is held to be a gateway between the human and spiritual realms. ==Festivals== ''[[Seimei Matsuri]]'' (晴明祭) is held every year on the [[September equinox|Autumnal equinox]]. == images == File:Seimei Shrine-3470.jpg|''Torii'' with pentacle File:Seimei Shrine-3476.jpg|Stone lion File:Seimei Shrine-3487.jpg|Stone ''[[komainu]]'' File:Seimei Shrine-3504.jpg|[[Chōzuya]] File:Seimei Shrine-3520.jpg|Front shrine File:Seimei Shrine-3522.jpg|Peach bust File:Seimei Shrine-3535.jpg|''Kusu-no-ki'', a sacred tree File:Seimei Shrine-3555.jpg|Stone tablet for Sen no Rikyū ==See also== *[[Abe no Seimei]] *[[Onmyōdō]] ==References== {{refbegin}} {{refend}} ==External links== *[http://www.seimeijinja.jp/ Official Site] (Japanese) {{commons Category|Seimei Jinja|Seimei Shrine}} {{Shinto shrine}}]] {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages that used to use Authority control]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:Onmyōdō]] [[Category:1007 establishments]] [[Category:1000s establishments in Japan]] [[Category:Abe clan shrines]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 1]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:mm]] [[Category:Seimei Shrine]] [[Category:Seimei Shrines]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia]] }} [[sn:Seimei Shrine]] [[en:Seimei Shrine]]

A nearby bridge, the ''Ichijō Modori-bashi'' is situated just south of the shrine, and is held to be a gateway between the human and spiritual realms. ==Festivals== ''[[Seimei Matsuri]]'' (晴明祭) is held every year on the [[September equinox|Autumnal equinox]]. == images == File:Seimei Shrine-3470.jpg|''Torii'' with pentacle File:Seimei Shrine-3476.jpg|Stone lion File:Seimei Shrine-3487.jpg|Stone ''[[komainu]]'' File:Seimei Shrine-3504.jpg|[[Chōzuya]] File:Seimei Shrine-3520.jpg|Front shrine File:Seimei Shrine-3522.jpg|Peach bust File:Seimei Shrine-3535.jpg|''Kusu-no-ki'', a sacred tree File:Seimei Shrine-3555.jpg|Stone tablet for Sen no Rikyū ==See also== *[[Abe no Seimei]] *[[Onmyōdō]] ==References== {{refbegin}} {{refend}} ==External links== *[http://www.seimeijinja.jp/ Official Site] (Japanese) {{commons Category|Seimei Jinja|Seimei Shrine}} {{Shinto shrine}}]] {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages that used to use Authority control]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:Onmyōdō]] [[Category:1007 establishments]] [[Category:1000s establishments in Japan]] [[Category:Abe clan shrines]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 1]] [[Category:mm]] [[Category:Seimei Shrines]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia]] }} [[sn:Seimei Shrine]] [[en:Seimei Shrine]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[Category:pages with valid en interwikis]]

{{short description|Shinto shrines in Kyoto, Japan}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Seimei Shrine
晴明神社 | image = Seimei Shrine-3483.jpg | caption = [[Torii]] gate at entrance to Seimei Shrine | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|35|01|40|N|135|45|04|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = [[Abe no Seimei]] | founded_by = [[Emperor Ichijō]] | established = 1007 | date_destroyed = | location = 806 Ichijō-agaru, Horikawa-dōri, [[Kamigyō-ku, Kyoto|Kamigyō-ku]], [[Kyoto|Kyōto-shi]], [[Kyoto Prefecture|Kyōto-ken]]
[[〒]] 602-8222 | website = {{URL|http://www.seimeijinja.jp/}} | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = }} {{nihongo|'''Seimei Shrine'''|晴明神社|Seimei-jinja|}} is a [[Shinto shrine]] that is located in [[Kyoto]], [[Japan]]. It is dedicated to the ''[[Onmyōdō|onmyōji]]'', [[Abe no Seimei]]. ==History== The Seimei Shrine was founded on the 4th year of the [[Kankō]] [[Japanese era|era]] (1007) by [[Emperor Ichijō]], who ordered the shrine built in memory of Abe no Seimei, after his death in 1005. It was constructed on the site of Abe no Seimei's house. The main building was restored in 1925. ==Architecture== [[File:Seimei Shrine-3500.jpg|thumb|190px|left|''Seimei-i'' well with pentagram motif]] Two [[torii]] gates lead up to the entrance of the shrine. A famous well (''Seimei-i'') is located on the shrine grounds, and the water drawn from it is considered somewhat magical. Tea master [[Sen no Rikyū]] was known to have brewed tea with the water from this well. [[File:Seimei Shrine-3481.jpg|thumb|150px|right|''Ichijō Modori-bashi'']] The [[pentacle]], known locally as a ''Seimei-star'', is an important symbol found on many parts of the shrine. Abe no Seimei reputedly came up with the arcane insignia in the 10th century, symbolizing the [[Wuxing (Chinese philosophy)|Five Chinese Elements]]. [[Platycodon grandiflorus|Japanese bellflower]] (''Platycodon grandiflorus'') ornamentation are found in decorative tiles and lanterns. The five petal tips of the flower are thought to represent the same ideal as the pentagram. A nearby bridge, the ''Ichijō Modori-bashi'' is situated just south of the shrine, and is held to be a gateway between the human and spiritual realms. ==Festivals== ''[[Seimei Matsuri]]'' (晴明祭) is held every year on the [[September equinox|Autumnal equinox]]. == images == File:Seimei Shrine-3470.jpg|''Torii'' with pentacle File:Seimei Shrine-3476.jpg|Stone lion File:Seimei Shrine-3487.jpg|Stone ''[[komainu]]'' File:Seimei Shrine-3504.jpg|[[Chōzuya]] File:Seimei Shrine-3520.jpg|Front shrine File:Seimei Shrine-3522.jpg|Peach bust File:Seimei Shrine-3535.jpg|''Kusu-no-ki'', a sacred tree File:Seimei Shrine-3555.jpg|Stone tablet for Sen no Rikyū ==See also== *[[Abe no Seimei]] *[[Onmyōdō]] ==References== {{refbegin}} {{refend}} ==External links== *[http://www.seimeijinja.jp/ Official Site] (Japanese) {{commons Category|Seimei Jinja|Seimei Shrine}} {{Shinto shrine}}]] {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages that used to use Authority control]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:Onmyōdō]] [[Category:1007 establishments]] [[Category:1000s establishments in Japan]] [[Category:Abe clan shrines]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 1]] [[Category:mm]] [[Category:Seimei Shrines]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia]] }} [[sn:Seimei Shrine]] [[en:Seimei Shrine]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[Category:pages with valid en interwikis]] [[arz:ضريح سيمى]] [[fr:Seimei-jinja]] [[ja:晴明神社]] [[my:ဆဲမဲနတ်ကျောင်း]] [[zh:晴明神社]]

[[〒]] 602-8222 | website = {{URL|http://www.seimeijinja.jp/}} | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = }} {{nihongo|'''Seimei Shrine'''|晴明神社|Seimei-jinja|}} is a [[Shinto shrine]] that is located in [[Kyoto]], [[Japan]]. It is dedicated to the ''[[Onmyōdō|onmyōji]]'', [[Abe no Seimei]]. ==History== The Seimei Shrine was founded on the 4th year of the [[Kankō]] [[Japanese era|era]] (1007) by [[:en:Emperor Ichijō|Emperor Ichijō]], who ordered the shrine built in memory of Abe no Seimei, after his death in 1005. It was constructed on the site of Abe no Seimei's house. The main building was restored in 1925. ==Architecture== [[File:Seimei Shrine-3500.jpg|thumb|190px|left|''Seimei-i'' well with pentagram motif]] Two [[torii]] gates lead up to the entrance of the shrine. A famous well (''Seimei-i'') is located on the shrine grounds, and the water drawn from it is considered somewhat magical. Tea master [[Sen no Rikyū]] was known to have brewed tea with the water from this well. [[File:Seimei Shrine-3481.jpg|thumb|150px|right|''Ichijō Modori-bashi'']] The [[pentacle]], known locally as a ''Seimei-star'', is an important symbol found on many parts of the shrine. Abe no Seimei reputedly came up with the arcane insignia in the 10th century, symbolizing the [[Wuxing (Chinese philosophy)|Five Chinese Elements]]. [[Platycodon grandiflorus|Japanese bellflower]] (''Platycodon grandiflorus'') ornamentation are found in decorative tiles and lanterns. The five petal tips of the flower are thought to represent the same ideal as the pentagram. A nearby bridge, the ''Ichijō Modori-bashi'' is situated just south of the shrine, and is held to be a gateway between the human and spiritual realms. ==Festivals== ''[[Seimei Matsuri]]'' (晴明祭) is held every year on the [[September equinox|Autumnal equinox]]. == images == File:Seimei Shrine-3470.jpg|''Torii'' with pentacle File:Seimei Shrine-3476.jpg|Stone lion File:Seimei Shrine-3487.jpg|Stone ''[[komainu]]'' File:Seimei Shrine-3504.jpg|[[Chōzuya]] File:Seimei Shrine-3520.jpg|Front shrine File:Seimei Shrine-3522.jpg|Peach bust File:Seimei Shrine-3535.jpg|''Kusu-no-ki'', a sacred tree File:Seimei Shrine-3555.jpg|Stone tablet for Sen no Rikyū ==See also== *[[Abe no Seimei]] *[[Onmyōdō]] ==References== {{refbegin}} {{refend}} ==External links== *[http://www.seimeijinja.jp/ Official Site] (Japanese) {{commons Category|Seimei Jinja|Seimei Shrine}} {{Shinto shrine}}]] {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:Onmyōdō]] [[Category:1007 establishments]] [[Category:Seimei Shrines]] }} [[sn:Seimei Shrine]] [[en:Seimei Shrine]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[Category:pages with valid en interwikis]] [[arz:ضريح سيمى]] [[fr:Seimei-jinja]] [[ja:晴明神社]] [[my:ဆဲမဲနတ်ကျောင်း]] [[zh:晴明神社]]

A nearby bridge, the ''Ichijō Modori-bashi'' is situated just south of the shrine, and is held to be a gateway between the human and spiritual realms. ==Festivals== ''[[Seimei Matsuri]]'' (晴明祭) is held every year on the [[September equinox|Autumnal equinox]]. == images == File:Seimei Shrine-3470.jpg|''Torii'' with pentacle File:Seimei Shrine-3476.jpg|Stone lion File:Seimei Shrine-3487.jpg|Stone ''[[komainu]]'' File:Seimei Shrine-3504.jpg|[[:en:Chōzuya|Chōzuya]] File:Seimei Shrine-3520.jpg|Front shrine File:Seimei Shrine-3522.jpg|Peach bust File:Seimei Shrine-3535.jpg|''Kusu-no-ki'', a sacred tree File:Seimei Shrine-3555.jpg|Stone tablet for Sen no Rikyū ==See also== *[[Abe no Seimei]] *[[Onmyōdō]] ==References== {{refbegin}} {{refend}} ==External links== *[http://www.seimeijinja.jp/ Official Site] (Japanese) {{commons Category|Seimei Jinja|Seimei Shrine}} {{Shinto shrine}}]] {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:Onmyōdō]] [[Category:1007 establishments]] [[Category:Seimei Shrines]] }} [[sn:Seimei Shrine]] [[en:Seimei Shrine]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[Category:pages with valid en interwikis]] [[arz:ضريح سيمى]] [[fr:Seimei-jinja]] [[ja:晴明神社]] [[my:ဆဲမဲနတ်ကျောင်း]] [[zh:晴明神社]]

[[:en:〒|〒]] 602-8222 | website = {{URL|http://www.seimeijinja.jp/}} | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = }} {{nihongo|'''Seimei Shrine'''|晴明神社|Seimei-jinja|}} is a [[Shinto shrine]] that is located in [[Kyoto]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. It is dedicated to the ''[[Onmyōdō|onmyōji]]'', [[Abe no Seimei]]. ==History== The Seimei Shrine was founded on the 4th year of the [[:en:Kankō|Kankō]] [[:en:Japanese era|era]] (1007) by [[:en:Emperor Ichijō|Emperor Ichijō]], who ordered the shrine built in memory of Abe no Seimei, after his death in 1005. It was constructed on the site of Abe no Seimei's house. The main building was restored in 1925. ==Architecture== [[File:Seimei Shrine-3500.jpg|thumb|190px|left|''Seimei-i'' well with pentagram motif]] Two [[torii]] gates lead up to the entrance of the shrine. A famous well (''Seimei-i'') is located on the shrine grounds, and the water drawn from it is considered somewhat magical. Tea master [[Sen no Rikyū]] was known to have brewed tea with the water from this well. [[File:Seimei Shrine-3481.jpg|thumb|150px|right|''Ichijō Modori-bashi'']] The [[pentacle]], known locally as a ''Seimei-star'', is an important symbol found on many parts of the shrine. Abe no Seimei reputedly came up with the arcane insignia in the 10th century, symbolizing the [[Wuxing (Chinese philosophy)|Five Chinese Elements]]. [[Platycodon grandiflorus|Japanese bellflower]] (''Platycodon grandiflorus'') ornamentation are found in decorative tiles and lanterns. The five petal tips of the flower are thought to represent the same ideal as the pentagram. A nearby bridge, the ''Ichijō Modori-bashi'' is situated just south of the shrine, and is held to be a gateway between the human and spiritual realms. ==Festivals== ''[[Seimei Matsuri]]'' (晴明祭) is held every year on the [[September equinox|Autumnal equinox]]. == images == File:Seimei Shrine-3470.jpg|''Torii'' with pentacle File:Seimei Shrine-3476.jpg|Stone lion File:Seimei Shrine-3487.jpg|Stone ''[[:en:Komainu|Komainu]]'' File:Seimei Shrine-3504.jpg|[[:en:Chōzuya|Chōzuya]] File:Seimei Shrine-3520.jpg|Front shrine File:Seimei Shrine-3522.jpg|Peach bust File:Seimei Shrine-3535.jpg|''Kusu-no-ki'', a sacred tree File:Seimei Shrine-3555.jpg|Stone tablet for Sen no Rikyū ==See also== *[[Abe no Seimei]] *[[Onmyōdō]] ==References== {{refbegin}} {{refend}} ==External links== *[http://www.seimeijinja.jp/ Official Site] (Japanese) {{commons Category|Seimei Jinja|Seimei Shrine}} {{Shinto shrine}}]] {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:Onmyōdō]] [[Category:1007 establishments]] [[Category:Seimei Shrines]] }} [[sn:Seimei Shrine]] [[en:Seimei Shrine]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[Category:pages with valid en interwikis]] [[arz:ضريح سيمى]] [[fr:Seimei-jinja]] [[ja:晴明神社]] [[my:ဆဲမဲနတ်ကျောင်း]] [[zh:晴明神社]]
