{{short description|Shinto shrines in Kyoto, Japan}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Seimei Shrine
晴明神社 | image = Seimei Shrine-3483.jpg | caption = [[Torii]] gate at entrance to Seimei Shrine | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|35|01|40|N|135|45|04|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = [[Abe no Seimei]] | founded_by = [[:en:Emperor Ichijō|Emperor Ichijō]] | established = 1007 | date_destroyed = | location = 806 Ichijō-agaru, Horikawa-dōri, [[:en:Kamigyō-ku, Kyoto|Kamigyō-ku]], [[:en:Kyoto|Kyōto-shi]], [[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyōto-ken]]
[[:en:〒|〒]] 602-8222 | website = {{URL|http://www.seimeijinja.jp/}} | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = }} {{nihongo|'''Seimei Shrine'''|晴明神社|Seimei-jinja|}} is a [[Shinto shrine]] that is located in [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. It is dedicated to the ''[[Onmyōdō|onmyōji]]'', [[Abe no Seimei]]. ==History== The Seimei Shrine was founded on the 4th year of the [[:en:Kankō|Kankō]] [[:en:Japanese era|era]] (1007) by [[:en:Emperor Ichijō|Emperor Ichijō]], who ordered the shrine built in memory of Abe no Seimei, after his death in 1005. It was constructed on the site of Abe no Seimei's house. The main building was restored in 1925. ==Architecture== [[File:Seimei Shrine-3500.jpg|thumb|190px|left|''Seimei-i'' well with pentagram motif]] Two [[torii]] gates lead up to the entrance of the shrine. A famous well (''Seimei-i'') is located on the shrine grounds, and the water drawn from it is considered somewhat magical. Tea master [[Sen no Rikyū]] was known to have brewed tea with the water from this well. [[File:Seimei Shrine-3481.jpg|thumb|150px|right|''Ichijō Modori-bashi'']] The [[:en:pentacle|pentacle]], known locally as a ''Seimei-star'', is an important symbol found on many parts of the shrine. Abe no Seimei reputedly came up with the arcane insignia in the 10th century, symbolizing the [[:en:Wuxing (Chinese philosophy)|Five Chinese Elements]]. [[:en:Platycodon grandiflorus|Japanese bellflower]] (''Platycodon grandiflorus'') ornamentation are found in decorative tiles and lanterns. The five petal tips of the flower are thought to represent the same ideal as the pentagram. A nearby bridge, the ''Ichijō Modori-bashi'' is situated just south of the shrine, and is held to be a gateway between the human and spiritual realms. ==Festivals== ''[[Seimei Matsuri]]'' (晴明祭) is held every year on the [[September equinox|Autumnal equinox]]. == images == File:Seimei Shrine-3470.jpg|''Torii'' with pentacle File:Seimei Shrine-3476.jpg|Stone lion File:Seimei Shrine-3487.jpg|Stone ''[[:en:Komainu|Komainu]]'' File:Seimei Shrine-3504.jpg|[[:en:Chōzuya|Chōzuya]] File:Seimei Shrine-3520.jpg|Front shrine File:Seimei Shrine-3522.jpg|Peach bust File:Seimei Shrine-3535.jpg|''Kusu-no-ki'', a sacred tree File:Seimei Shrine-3555.jpg|Stone tablet for Sen no Rikyū ==See also== *[[Abe no Seimei]] *[[Onmyōdō]] ==References== {{refbegin}} {{refend}} ==External links== *[http://www.seimeijinja.jp/ Official Site] (Japanese) {{commons Category|Seimei Jinja|Seimei Shrine}} {{Shinto shrine}}]] {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:Onmyōdō]] [[Category:1007 establishments]] [[Category:Seimei Shrines]] }} [[sn:Seimei Shrine]] [[en:Seimei Shrine]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[arz:ضريح سيمى]] [[fr:Seimei-jinja]] [[ja:晴明神社]] [[my:ဆဲမဲနတ်ကျောင်း]] [[zh:晴明神社]]

晴明神社 | image = File:Seimei Jinja, Worship Place and Main Sanctuary 002.jpg | caption = [[Torii]] gate at entrance to Seimei Shrine | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{Coord|35.02775|N|135.75102777777778|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = {{ill|Abe no Seimei|qid=Q1150151}} | founded_by = [[:en:Emperor Ichijō|Emperor Ichijō]] | established = 1007 | date_destroyed = | location = 806 Ichijō-agaru, Horikawa-dōri, [[:en:Kamigyō-ku, Kyoto|Kamigyō-ku]], [[:en:Kyoto|Kyōto-shi]], [[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyōto-ken]]
[[:en:〒|〒]] 602-8222 | website = {{URL|http://www.seimeijinja.jp/}} | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = | prefecture = {{ill|Kamigyō-ku|qid=Q1321824}} }} {{nihongo|'''Seimei Shrine'''|晴明神社|Seimei-jinja|}} is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] that is located in [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. It is dedicated to the ''[[Onmyōdō|onmyōji]]'', [[Abe no Seimei]]. ==History== The Seimei Shrine was founded on the 4th year of the [[:en:Kankō|Kankō]] [[:en:Japanese era|era]] (1007) by [[:en:Emperor Ichijō|Emperor Ichijō]], who ordered the shrine built in memory of Abe no Seimei, after his death in 1005. It was constructed on the site of Abe no Seimei's house. The main building was restored in 1925. ==Architecture== [[File:Seimei Shrine-3500.jpg|thumb|190px|left|''Seimei-i'' well with pentagram motif]] Two [[torii]] gates lead up to the entrance of the shrine. A famous well (''Seimei-i'') is located on the shrine grounds, and the water drawn from it is considered somewhat magical. Tea master [[:en:Sen no Rikyū|Sen no Rikyū]] was known to have brewed tea with the water from this well. [[File:Seimei Shrine-3481.jpg|thumb|150px|right|''Ichijō Modori-bashi'']] The [[:en:pentacle|pentacle]], known locally as a ''Seimei-star'', is an important symbol found on many parts of the shrine. Abe no Seimei reputedly came up with the arcane insignia in the 10th century, symbolizing the [[:en:Wuxing (Chinese philosophy)|Five Chinese Elements]]. [[:en:Platycodon grandiflorus|Japanese bellflower]] (''Platycodon grandiflorus'') ornamentation are found in decorative tiles and lanterns. The five petal tips of the flower are thought to represent the same ideal as the pentagram. A nearby bridge, the ''Ichijō Modori-bashi'' is situated just south of the shrine, and is held to be a gateway between the human and spiritual realms. ==Festivals== ''[[Seimei Matsuri]]'' (晴明祭) is held every year on the [[:en:September equinox|Autumnal equinox]]. == images == File:Seimei Shrine-3470.jpg|''Torii'' with pentacle File:Seimei Shrine-3476.jpg|Stone lion File:Seimei Shrine-3487.jpg|Stone ''[[:en:Komainu|Komainu]]'' File:Seimei Shrine-3504.jpg|[[:en:Chōzuya|Chōzuya]] File:Seimei Shrine-3520.jpg|Front shrine File:Seimei Shrine-3522.jpg|Peach bust File:Seimei Shrine-3535.jpg|''Kusu-no-ki'', a sacred tree File:Seimei Shrine-3555.jpg|Stone tablet for Sen no Rikyū ==See also== *[[Abe no Seimei]] *[[Onmyōdō]] ==References== {{refbegin}} {{refend}} ==External links== *[http://www.seimeijinja.jp/ Official Site] (Japanese) {{commons Category|Seimei Jinja|Seimei Shrine}} ]] {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:Onmyōdō]] [[Category:1007 establishments]] [[Category:Seimei Shrines]] }} [[sn:Seimei Shrine]] [[en:Seimei Shrine]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[arz:ضريح سيمى]] [[fr:Seimei-jinja]] [[ja:晴明神社]] [[my:ဆဲမဲနတ်ကျောင်း]] [[zh:晴明神社]]

It was constructed on the site of Abe no Seimei's house. [[:en:Platycodon grandiflorus|Japanese bellflower]] (''Platycodon grandiflorus'') ornamentation are found in decorative tiles and lanterns. A nearby bridge, the ''Ichijō Modori-bashi'' is situated just south of the shrine, and is held to be a gateway between the human and spiritual realms. ==Festivals== ''[[Seimei Matsuri]]'' (晴明祭) is held every year on the [[:en:September equinox|Autumnal equinox]]. == images == File:Seimei Shrine-3470.jpg|''Torii'' with pentacle File:Seimei Shrine-3476.jpg|Stone lion File:Seimei Shrine-3487.jpg|Stone ''[[:en:Komainu|Komainu]]'' File:Seimei Shrine-3504.jpg|[[:en:Chōzuya|Chōzuya]] File:Seimei Shrine-3520.jpg|Front shrine File:Seimei Shrine-3522.jpg|Peach bust File:Seimei Shrine-3535.jpg|''Kusu-no-ki'', a sacred tree File:Seimei Shrine-3555.jpg|Stone tablet for Sen no Rikyū ==See also== *[[Abe no Seimei]] *[[Onmyōdō]] ==References== {{refbegin}} {{refend}} ==External links== *[http://www.seimeijinja.jp/ Official Site] (Japanese) {{commons Category|Seimei Jinja|Seimei Shrine}} ]] {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:Onmyōdō]] [[Category:1007 establishments]] [[Category:Seimei Shrines]] }} [[en:Seimei Shrine]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[arz:ضريح سيمى]] [[fr:Seimei-jinja]] [[ja:晴明神社]] [[my:ဆဲမဲနတ်ကျောင်း]] [[zh:晴明神社]] {{wikidata link|Q276830}}

晴明神社 | image = File:Seimei Jinja, Worship Place and Main Sanctuary 002.jpg | caption = [[Torii]] gate at entrance to Seimei Shrine | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{Coord|35.02775|N|135.75102777777778|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = {{ill|Abe no Seimei|WD=Q1150151}} | founded_by = [[:en:Emperor Ichijō|Emperor Ichijō]] | established = 1007 | date_destroyed = | location = 806 Ichijō-agaru, Horikawa-dōri, [[:en:Kamigyō-ku, Kyoto|Kamigyō-ku]], [[:en:Kyoto|Kyōto-shi]], [[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyōto-ken]]
[[:en:〒|〒]] 602-8222 | website = {{URL|http://www.seimeijinja.jp/}} | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = | location = {{ill|Kamigyō-ku|WD=Q1321824}} }} {{nihongo|'''Seimei Shrine'''|晴明神社|Seimei-jinja|}} is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] that is located in [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. It is dedicated to the ''[[Onmyōdō|onmyōji]]'', [[Abe no Seimei]]. ==History== The Seimei Shrine was founded on the 4th year of the [[:en:Kankō|Kankō]] [[:en:Japanese era|era]] (1007) by [[:en:Emperor Ichijō|Emperor Ichijō]], who ordered the shrine built in memory of Abe no Seimei, after his death in 1005. It was constructed on the site of Abe no Seimei's house. The main building was restored in 1925. ==Architecture== [[File:Seimei Shrine-3500.jpg|thumb|190px|left|''Seimei-i'' well with pentagram motif]] Two [[torii]] gates lead up to the entrance of the shrine. A famous well (''Seimei-i'') is located on the shrine grounds, and the water drawn from it is considered somewhat magical. Tea master [[:en:Sen no Rikyū|Sen no Rikyū]] was known to have brewed tea with the water from this well. [[File:Seimei Shrine-3481.jpg|thumb|150px|right|''Ichijō Modori-bashi'']] The [[:en:pentacle|pentacle]], known locally as a ''Seimei-star'', is an important symbol found on many parts of the shrine. Abe no Seimei reputedly came up with the arcane insignia in the 10th century, symbolizing the [[:en:Wuxing (Chinese philosophy)|Five Chinese Elements]]. [[:en:Platycodon grandiflorus|Japanese bellflower]] (''Platycodon grandiflorus'') ornamentation are found in decorative tiles and lanterns. The five petal tips of the flower are thought to represent the same ideal as the pentagram. A nearby bridge, the ''Ichijō Modori-bashi'' is situated just south of the shrine, and is held to be a gateway between the human and spiritual realms. ==Festivals== ''[[Seimei Matsuri]]'' (晴明祭) is held every year on the [[:en:September equinox|Autumnal equinox]]. == images == File:Seimei Shrine-3470.jpg|''Torii'' with pentacle File:Seimei Shrine-3476.jpg|Stone lion File:Seimei Shrine-3487.jpg|Stone ''[[:en:Komainu|Komainu]]'' File:Seimei Shrine-3504.jpg|[[:en:Chōzuya|Chōzuya]] File:Seimei Shrine-3520.jpg|Front shrine File:Seimei Shrine-3522.jpg|Peach bust File:Seimei Shrine-3535.jpg|''Kusu-no-ki'', a sacred tree File:Seimei Shrine-3555.jpg|Stone tablet for Sen no Rikyū ==See also== *[[Abe no Seimei]] *[[Onmyōdō]] ==References== {{refbegin}} {{refend}} ==External links== *[http://www.seimeijinja.jp/ Official Site] (Japanese) {{commons Category|Seimei Jinja|Seimei Shrine}} ]] {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:Onmyōdō]] [[Category:1007 establishments]] [[Category:Seimei Shrines]] }} [[en:Seimei Shrine]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[arz:ضريح سيمى]] [[fr:Seimei-jinja]] [[ja:晴明神社]] [[my:ဆဲမဲနတ်ကျောင်း]] [[zh:晴明神社]] {{wikidata link|Q276830}}

[[arz:ضريح سيمى]][[en:Seimei Shrine]][[fr:Seimei-jinja]][[ja:晴明神社]][[my:ဆဲမဲနတ်ကျောင်း]][[zh:晴明神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q276830]] Wikidata name: Seimei Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:09, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{short description|Shinto shrines in Kyoto, Japan}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Seimei Shrine
晴明神社 | image = File:Seimei Jinja, Worship Place and Main Sanctuary 002.jpg | caption = [[Torii]] gate at entrance to Seimei Shrine | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{Coord|35.02775|N|135.75102777777778|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = {{ill|Abe no Seimei|WD=Q1150151}} | founded_by = [[:en:Emperor Ichijō|Emperor Ichijō]] | established = 1007 | date_destroyed = | location = 806 Ichijō-agaru, Horikawa-dōri, [[:en:Kamigyō-ku, Kyoto|Kamigyō-ku]], [[:en:Kyoto|Kyōto-shi]], [[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyōto-ken]]
[[:en:〒|〒]] 602-8222 | website = {{URL|http://www.seimeijinja.jp/}} | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = | location = {{ill|Kamigyō-ku|WD=Q1321824}} }} {{nihongo|'''Seimei Shrine'''|晴明神社|Seimei-jinja|}} is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] that is located in [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. It is dedicated to the ''[[Onmyōdō|onmyōji]]'', [[Abe no Seimei]]. ==History== The Seimei Shrine was founded on the 4th year of the [[:en:Kankō|Kankō]] [[:en:Japanese era|era]] (1007) by [[:en:Emperor Ichijō|Emperor Ichijō]], who ordered the shrine built in memory of Abe no Seimei, after his death in 1005. It was constructed on the site of Abe no Seimei's house. The main building was restored in 1925. ==Architecture== [[File:Seimei Shrine-3500.jpg|thumb|190px|left|''Seimei-i'' well with pentagram motif]] Two [[torii]] gates lead up to the entrance of the shrine. A famous well (''Seimei-i'') is located on the shrine grounds, and the water drawn from it is considered somewhat magical. Tea master [[:en:Sen no Rikyū|Sen no Rikyū]] was known to have brewed tea with the water from this well. [[File:Seimei Shrine-3481.jpg|thumb|150px|right|''Ichijō Modori-bashi'']] The [[:en:pentacle|pentacle]], known locally as a ''Seimei-star'', is an important symbol found on many parts of the shrine. Abe no Seimei reputedly came up with the arcane insignia in the 10th century, symbolizing the [[:en:Wuxing (Chinese philosophy)|Five Chinese Elements]]. [[:en:Platycodon grandiflorus|Japanese bellflower]] (''Platycodon grandiflorus'') ornamentation are found in decorative tiles and lanterns. The five petal tips of the flower are thought to represent the same ideal as the pentagram. A nearby bridge, the ''Ichijō Modori-bashi'' is situated just south of the shrine, and is held to be a gateway between the human and spiritual realms. ==Festivals== ''[[Seimei Matsuri]]'' (晴明祭) is held every year on the [[:en:September equinox|Autumnal equinox]]. == images == File:Seimei Shrine-3470.jpg|''Torii'' with pentacle File:Seimei Shrine-3476.jpg|Stone lion File:Seimei Shrine-3487.jpg|Stone ''[[:en:Komainu|Komainu]]'' File:Seimei Shrine-3504.jpg|[[:en:Chōzuya|Chōzuya]] File:Seimei Shrine-3520.jpg|Front shrine File:Seimei Shrine-3522.jpg|Peach bust File:Seimei Shrine-3535.jpg|''Kusu-no-ki'', a sacred tree File:Seimei Shrine-3555.jpg|Stone tablet for Sen no Rikyū ==See also== *[[Abe no Seimei]] *[[Onmyōdō]] ==References== {{refbegin}} {{refend}} ==External links== *[http://www.seimeijinja.jp/ Official Site] (Japanese) {{commons Category|Seimei Jinja|Seimei Shrine}} ]] {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:Onmyōdō]] [[Category:1007 establishments]] [[Category:Seimei Shrines]] }} [[en:Seimei Shrine]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[arz:ضريح سيمى]] [[fr:Seimei-jinja]] [[ja:晴明神社]] [[my:ဆဲမဲနတ်ကျောင်း]] [[zh:晴明神社]] {{wikidata link|Q276830}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

A nearby bridge, the ''Ichijō Modori-bashi'' is situated just south of the shrine, and is held to be a gateway between the human and spiritual realms. ==Festivals== ''[[Seimei Matsuri]]'' (晴明祭) is held every year on the [[:en:September equinox|Autumnal equinox]]. == images == File:Seimei Shrine-3470.jpg|''Torii'' with pentacle File:Seimei Shrine-3476.jpg|Stone lion File:Seimei Shrine-3487.jpg|Stone ''[[:en:Komainu|Komainu]]'' File:Seimei Shrine-3504.jpg|[[:en:Chōzuya|Chōzuya]] File:Seimei Shrine-3520.jpg|Front shrine File:Seimei Shrine-3522.jpg|Peach bust File:Seimei Shrine-3535.jpg|''Kusu-no-ki'', a sacred tree File:Seimei Shrine-3555.jpg|Stone tablet for Sen no Rikyū ==See also== *[[Abe no Seimei]] *[[Onmyōdō]] ==References== {{refbegin}} {{refend}} ==External links== *[http://www.seimeijinja.jp/ Official Site] (Japanese) {{commons Category|Seimei Jinja|Seimei Shrine}} ]] {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:Onmyōdō]] [[Category:1007 establishments]] [[Category:Seimei Shrines]] }} [[en:Seimei Shrine]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[arz:ضريح سيمى]] [[fr:Seimei-jinja]] [[ja:晴明神社]] [[my:ဆဲမဲနတ်ကျောင်း]] [[zh:晴明神社]] {{wikidata link|Q276830}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]]

晴明神社 | image = File:Seimei Jinja, Worship Place and Main Sanctuary 002.jpg | caption = [[Torii]] gate at entrance to Seimei Shrine | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{Coord|35.02775|N|135.75102777777778|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = {{ill|Abe no Seimei|WD=Q1150151}} | founded_by = [[:en:Emperor Ichijō|Emperor Ichijō]] | established = 1007 | date_destroyed = | location = 806 Ichijō-agaru, Horikawa-dōri, [[:en:Kamigyō-ku, Kyoto|Kamigyō-ku]], [[:en:Kyoto|Kyōto-shi]], [[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyōto-ken]]
[[:en:〒|〒]] 602-8222 | website = {{URL|http://www.seimeijinja.jp/}} | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = | location = {{ill|Kamigyō-ku|WD=Q1321824}} }} {{nihongo|'''Seimei Shrine'''|晴明神社|Seimei-jinja|}} is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] that is located in [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. It is dedicated to the ''[[Onmyōdō|onmyōji]]'', [[Abe no Seimei]]. ==History== The Seimei Shrine was founded on the 4th year of the [[:en:Kankō|Kankō]] [[:en:Japanese era|era]] (1007) by [[:en:Emperor Ichijō|Emperor Ichijō]], who ordered the shrine built in memory of Abe no Seimei, after his death in 1005. It was constructed on the site of Abe no Seimei's house. The main building was restored in 1925. ==Architecture== [[File:Seimei Shrine-3500.jpg|thumb|190px|left|''Seimei-i'' well with pentagram motif]] Two [[torii]] gates lead up to the entrance of the shrine. A famous well (''Seimei-i'') is located on the shrine grounds, and the water drawn from it is considered somewhat magical. Tea master [[:en:Sen no Rikyū|Sen no Rikyū]] was known to have brewed tea with the water from this well. [[File:Seimei Shrine-3481.jpg|thumb|150px|right|''Ichijō Modori-bashi'']] The [[:en:pentacle|pentacle]], known locally as a ''Seimei-star'', is an important symbol found on many parts of the shrine. Abe no Seimei reputedly came up with the arcane insignia in the 10th century, symbolizing the [[:en:Wuxing (Chinese philosophy)|Five Chinese Elements]]. [[:en:Platycodon grandiflorus|Japanese bellflower]] (''Platycodon grandiflorus'') ornamentation are found in decorative tiles and lanterns. The five petal tips of the flower are thought to represent the same ideal as the pentagram. A nearby bridge, the ''Ichijō Modori-bashi'' is situated just south of the shrine, and is held to be a gateway between the human and spiritual realms. ==Festivals== ''[[Seimei Matsuri]]'' (晴明祭) is held every year on the [[:en:September equinox|Autumnal equinox]]. == images == File:Seimei Shrine-3470.jpg|''Torii'' with pentacle File:Seimei Shrine-3476.jpg|Stone lion File:Seimei Shrine-3487.jpg|Stone ''[[:en:Komainu|Komainu]]'' File:Seimei Shrine-3504.jpg|[[:en:Chōzuya|Chōzuya]] File:Seimei Shrine-3520.jpg|Front shrine File:Seimei Shrine-3522.jpg|Peach bust File:Seimei Shrine-3535.jpg|''Kusu-no-ki'', a sacred tree File:Seimei Shrine-3555.jpg|Stone tablet for Sen no Rikyū ==See also== *[[Abe no Seimei]] *[[Onmyōdō]] ==References== {{refbegin}} {{refend}} ==External links== *[http://www.seimeijinja.jp/ Official Site] (Japanese) {{commons Category|Seimei Jinja|Seimei Shrine}} ]] {{draft categories| }} [[en:Seimei Shrine]] [[arz:ضريح سيمى]] [[fr:Seimei-jinja]] [[ja:晴明神社]] [[my:ဆဲမဲနတ်ကျောင်း]] [[zh:晴明神社]] {{wikidata link|Q276830}} [[Category:1007 establishments]] [[Category:Onmyōdō]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[Category:Seimei Shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

A famous well (''Seimei-i'') is located on the shrine grounds, and the water drawn from it is considered somewhat magical. Tea master [[:en:Sen no Rikyū|Sen no Rikyū]] was known to have brewed tea with the water from this well. [[File:Seimei Shrine-3481.jpg|thumb|150px|right|''Ichijō Modori-bashi'']] The [[:en:pentacle|pentacle]], known locally as a ''Seimei-star'', is an important symbol found on many parts of the shrine. Abe no Seimei reputedly came up with the arcane insignia in the 10th century, symbolizing the [[:en:Wuxing (Chinese philosophy)|Five Chinese Elements]]. [[:en:Platycodon grandiflorus|Japanese bellflower]] (''Platycodon grandiflorus'') ornamentation are found in decorative tiles and lanterns. The five petal tips of the flower are thought to represent the same ideal as the pentagram. A nearby bridge, the ''Ichijō Modori-bashi'' is situated just south of the shrine, and is held to be a gateway between the human and spiritual realms. ==Festivals== ''[[Seimei Matsuri]]'' (晴明祭) is held every year on the [[:en:September equinox|Autumnal equinox]]. == images == File:Seimei Shrine-3470.jpg|''Torii'' with pentacle File:Seimei Shrine-3476.jpg|Stone lion File:Seimei Shrine-3487.jpg|Stone ''[[:en:Komainu|Komainu]]'' File:Seimei Shrine-3504.jpg|[[:en:Chōzuya|Chōzuya]] File:Seimei Shrine-3520.jpg|Front shrine File:Seimei Shrine-3522.jpg|Peach bust File:Seimei Shrine-3535.jpg|''Kusu-no-ki'', a sacred tree File:Seimei Shrine-3555.jpg|Stone tablet for Sen no Rikyū ==See also== *[[Abe no Seimei]] *[[Onmyōdō]] ==References== {{refbegin}} {{refend}} ==External links== *[http://www.seimeijinja.jp/ Official Site] (Japanese) {{commons Category|Seimei Jinja|Seimei Shrine}} ]] [[en:Seimei Shrine]] [[arz:ضريح سيمى]] [[fr:Seimei-jinja]] [[ja:晴明神社]] [[my:ဆဲမဲနတ်ကျောင်း]] [[zh:晴明神社]] {{wikidata link|Q276830}} [[Category:1007 establishments]] [[Category:Onmyōdō]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[Category:Seimei Shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Pages with unclosed square brackets]]

A nearby bridge, the ''Ichijō Modori-bashi'' is situated just south of the shrine, and is held to be a gateway between the human and spiritual realms. ==Festivals== ''[[Seimei Matsuri]]'' (晴明祭) is held every year on the {{ill|September equinox|en|September equinox|WD=Q7530126|lt=Autumnal equinox}}. == images == File:Seimei Shrine-3470.jpg|''Torii'' with pentacle File:Seimei Shrine-3476.jpg|Stone lion File:Seimei Shrine-3487.jpg|Stone ''{{ill|Komainu|en|Komainu|WD=Q20155846|lt=Komainu}}'' File:Seimei Shrine-3504.jpg|{{ill|Chōzuya|en|Chōzuya|WD=Q1069667|lt=Chōzuya}} File:Seimei Shrine-3520.jpg|Front shrine File:Seimei Shrine-3522.jpg|Peach bust File:Seimei Shrine-3535.jpg|''Kusu-no-ki'', a sacred tree File:Seimei Shrine-3555.jpg|Stone tablet for Sen no Rikyū ==See also== *[[Abe no Seimei]] *[[Onmyōdō]] ==References== {{refbegin}} {{refend}} ==External links== *[http://www.seimeijinja.jp/ Official Site] (Japanese) {{commons Category|Seimei Jinja|Seimei Shrine}} ]] [[en:Seimei Shrine]] [[arz:ضريح سيمى]] [[fr:Seimei-jinja]] [[ja:晴明神社]] [[my:ဆဲမဲနတ်ကျောင်း]] [[zh:晴明神社]] {{wikidata link|Q276830}} [[Category:1007 establishments]] [[Category:Onmyōdō]] [[Category:Seimei Shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Pages with unclosed square brackets]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]]

revision.text:
[[arz:ضريح سيمى]][[en:Seimei Shrine]][[fr:Seimei-jinja]][[ja:晴明神社]][[my:ဆဲမဲနတ်ကျောင်း]][[zh:晴明神社]] [[arz:ضريح سيمى]][[en:Seimei Shrine]][[fr:Seimei-jinja]][[ja:晴明神社]][[my:ဆဲမဲနတ်ကျောင်း]][[zh:晴明神社]] [[arz:ضريح سيمى]][[en:Seimei Shrine]][[fr:Seimei-jinja]][[ja:晴明神社]][[my:ဆဲမဲနတ်ကျောင်း]][[zh:晴明神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q276830]] Wikidata name: Seimei Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:09, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{short description|Shinto shrines in Kyoto, Japan}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Seimei Shrine
晴明神社 | image = File:Seimei Jinja, Worship Place and Main Sanctuary 002.jpg | caption = [[Torii]] gate at entrance to Seimei Shrine | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{Coord|35.02775|N|135.75102777777778|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = {{ill|Abe no Seimei|WD=Q1150151}} | founded_by = {{ill|Emperor Ichijō|en|Emperor Ichijō|WD=Q449540|lt=Emperor Ichijō}} | established = 1007 | date_destroyed = | location = 806 Ichijō-agaru, Horikawa-dōri, {{ill|Kamigyō-ku, Kyoto|en|Kamigyō-ku, Kyoto|WD=Q1321824|lt=Kamigyō-ku}}, {{ill|Kyoto|en|Kyoto|WD=Q34600|lt=Kyōto-shi}}, {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|en|Kyoto Prefecture|WD=Q120730|lt=Kyōto-ken}}
[[:en:〒|〒]] 602-8222 | website = {{URL|http://www.seimeijinja.jp/}} | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = | location = {{ill|Kamigyō-ku|WD=Q1321824}} }} {{nihongo|'''Seimei Shrine'''|晴明神社|Seimei-jinja|}} is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} that is located in {{ill|Kyoto|en|Kyoto|WD=Q34600|lt=Kyoto}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}. It is dedicated to the ''[[Onmyōdō|onmyōji]]'', [[Abe no Seimei]]. ==History== The Seimei Shrine was founded on the 4th year of the {{ill|Kankō|en|Kankō|WD=Q2736722|lt=Kankō}} [[:en:Japanese era|era]] (1007) by {{ill|Emperor Ichijō|en|Emperor Ichijō|WD=Q449540|lt=Emperor Ichijō}}, who ordered the shrine built in memory of Abe no Seimei, after his death in 1005. It was constructed on the site of Abe no Seimei's house. The main building was restored in 1925. ==Architecture== [[File:Seimei Shrine-3500.jpg|thumb|190px|left|''Seimei-i'' well with pentagram motif]] Two [[torii]] gates lead up to the entrance of the shrine. A famous well (''Seimei-i'') is located on the shrine grounds, and the water drawn from it is considered somewhat magical. Tea master {{ill|Sen no Rikyū|en|Sen no Rikyū|WD=Q305477|lt=Sen no Rikyū}} was known to have brewed tea with the water from this well. [[File:Seimei Shrine-3481.jpg|thumb|150px|right|''Ichijō Modori-bashi'']] The {{ill|pentacle|en|pentacle|WD=Q2642650|lt=pentacle}}, known locally as a ''Seimei-star'', is an important symbol found on many parts of the shrine. Abe no Seimei reputedly came up with the arcane insignia in the 10th century, symbolizing the {{ill|Wuxing (Chinese philosophy)|en|Wuxing (Chinese philosophy)|WD=Q218652|lt=Five Chinese Elements}}. [[:en:Platycodon grandiflorus|Japanese bellflower]] (''Platycodon grandiflorus'') ornamentation are found in decorative tiles and lanterns. The five petal tips of the flower are thought to represent the same ideal as the pentagram. A nearby bridge, the ''Ichijō Modori-bashi'' is situated just south of the shrine, and is held to be a gateway between the human and spiritual realms. ==Festivals== ''[[Seimei Matsuri]]'' (晴明祭) is held every year on the {{ill|September equinox|en|September equinox|WD=Q7530126|lt=Autumnal equinox}}. == images == File:Seimei Shrine-3470.jpg|''Torii'' with pentacle File:Seimei Shrine-3476.jpg|Stone lion File:Seimei Shrine-3487.jpg|Stone ''{{ill|Komainu|en|Komainu|WD=Q20155846|lt=Komainu}}'' File:Seimei Shrine-3504.jpg|{{ill|Chōzuya|en|Chōzuya|WD=Q1069667|lt=Chōzuya}} File:Seimei Shrine-3520.jpg|Front shrine File:Seimei Shrine-3522.jpg|Peach bust File:Seimei Shrine-3535.jpg|''Kusu-no-ki'', a sacred tree File:Seimei Shrine-3555.jpg|Stone tablet for Sen no Rikyū ==See also== *[[Abe no Seimei]] *[[Onmyōdō]] ==References== {{refbegin}} {{refend}} ==External links== *[http://www.seimeijinja.jp/ Official Site] (Japanese) {{commons Category|Seimei Jinja|Seimei Shrine}} ]] [[en:Seimei Shrine]] [[arz:ضريح سيمى]] [[fr:Seimei-jinja]] [[ja:晴明神社]] [[my:ဆဲမဲနတ်ကျောင်း]] [[zh:晴明神社]] {{wikidata link|Q276830}} [[Category:1007 establishments]] [[Category:Onmyōdō]] [[Category:Seimei Shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Pages with unclosed square brackets]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]]

revision.text:
[[arz:ضريح سيمى]][[en:Seimei Shrine]][[fr:Seimei-jinja]][[ja:晴明神社]][[my:ဆဲမဲနတ်ကျောင်း]][[zh:晴明神社]] [[arz:ضريح سيمى]][[en:Seimei Shrine]][[fr:Seimei-jinja]][[ja:晴明神社]][[my:ဆဲမဲနတ်ကျောင်း]][[zh:晴明神社]] [[arz:ضريح سيمى]][[en:Seimei Shrine]][[fr:Seimei-jinja]][[ja:晴明神社]][[my:ဆဲမဲနတ်ကျောင်း]][[zh:晴明神社]] {{short description|Shinto shrines in Kyoto, Japan}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Seimei Shrine
晴明神社 | image = File:Seimei Jinja, Worship Place and Main Sanctuary 002.jpg | caption = [[Torii]] gate at entrance to Seimei Shrine | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{Coord|35.02775|N|135.75102777777778|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = {{ill|Abe no Seimei|WD=Q1150151|1=Abe no Seimei}} | founded_by = {{ill|Emperor Ichijō|en|Emperor Ichijō|WD=Q449540|lt=Emperor Ichijō|1=Emperor Ichijō}} | established = 1007 | date_destroyed = | location = 806 Ichijō-agaru, Horikawa-dōri, {{ill|Kamigyō-ku, Kyoto|en|Kamigyō-ku, Kyoto|WD=Q1321824|lt=Kamigyō-ku|1=Kamigyō-ku, Kyoto}}, {{ill|Kyoto|en|Kyoto|WD=Q34600|lt=Kyōto-shi|1=Kyoto}}, {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|en|Kyoto Prefecture|WD=Q120730|lt=Kyōto-ken|1=Kyoto Prefecture}}
[[:en:〒|〒]] 602-8222 | website = {{URL|http://www.seimeijinja.jp/}} | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = | location = {{ill|Kamigyō-ku|WD=Q1321824|1=Kamigyō-ku, Kyoto}} }} {{nihongo|'''Seimei Shrine'''|晴明神社|Seimei-jinja|}} is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} that is located in {{ill|Kyoto|en|Kyoto|WD=Q34600|lt=Kyoto|1=Kyoto}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. It is dedicated to the ''[[Onmyōdō|onmyōji]]'', [[Abe no Seimei]]. ==History== The Seimei Shrine was founded on the 4th year of the {{ill|Kankō|en|Kankō|WD=Q2736722|lt=Kankō|1=Kankō}} [[:en:Japanese era|era]] (1007) by {{ill|Emperor Ichijō|en|Emperor Ichijō|WD=Q449540|lt=Emperor Ichijō|1=Emperor Ichijō}}, who ordered the shrine built in memory of Abe no Seimei, after his death in 1005. It was constructed on the site of Abe no Seimei's house. The main building was restored in 1925. ==Architecture== [[File:Seimei Shrine-3500.jpg|thumb|190px|left|''Seimei-i'' well with pentagram motif]] Two [[torii]] gates lead up to the entrance of the shrine. A famous well (''Seimei-i'') is located on the shrine grounds, and the water drawn from it is considered somewhat magical. Tea master {{ill|Sen no Rikyū|en|Sen no Rikyū|WD=Q305477|lt=Sen no Rikyū|1=Sen no Rikyū}} was known to have brewed tea with the water from this well. [[File:Seimei Shrine-3481.jpg|thumb|150px|right|''Ichijō Modori-bashi'']] The {{ill|pentacle|en|pentacle|WD=Q2642650|lt=pentacle|1=Pentacle}}, known locally as a ''Seimei-star'', is an important symbol found on many parts of the shrine. Abe no Seimei reputedly came up with the arcane insignia in the 10th century, symbolizing the {{ill|Wuxing (Chinese philosophy)|en|Wuxing (Chinese philosophy)|WD=Q218652|lt=Five Chinese Elements|1=Wuxing (Chinese philosophy)}}. [[:en:Platycodon grandiflorus|Japanese bellflower]] (''Platycodon grandiflorus'') ornamentation are found in decorative tiles and lanterns. The five petal tips of the flower are thought to represent the same ideal as the pentagram. A nearby bridge, the ''Ichijō Modori-bashi'' is situated just south of the shrine, and is held to be a gateway between the human and spiritual realms. ==Festivals== ''[[Seimei Matsuri]]'' (晴明祭) is held every year on the {{ill|September equinox|en|September equinox|WD=Q7530126|lt=Autumnal equinox|1=September equinox}}. == images == File:Seimei Shrine-3470.jpg|''Torii'' with pentacle File:Seimei Shrine-3476.jpg|Stone lion File:Seimei Shrine-3487.jpg|Stone ''{{ill|Komainu|en|Komainu|WD=Q20155846|lt=Komainu|1=Komainu}}'' File:Seimei Shrine-3504.jpg|{{ill|Chōzuya|en|Chōzuya|WD=Q1069667|lt=Chōzuya|1=Chōzuya}} File:Seimei Shrine-3520.jpg|Front shrine File:Seimei Shrine-3522.jpg|Peach bust File:Seimei Shrine-3535.jpg|''Kusu-no-ki'', a sacred tree File:Seimei Shrine-3555.jpg|Stone tablet for Sen no Rikyū ==See also== *[[Abe no Seimei]] *[[Onmyōdō]] ==References== {{refbegin}} {{refend}} ==External links== *[http://www.seimeijinja.jp/ Official Site] (Japanese) {{commons Category|Seimei Jinja|Seimei Shrine}} ]] [[en:Seimei Shrine]] [[arz:ضريح سيمى]] [[fr:Seimei-jinja]] [[ja:晴明神社]] [[my:ဆဲမဲနတ်ကျောင်း]] [[zh:晴明神社]] {{wikidata link|Q276830}} [[Category:1007 establishments]] [[Category:Onmyōdō]] [[Category:Seimei Shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Pages with unclosed square brackets]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]]
